maps.adminUx: Single admin page (`/ws/:id/seo-strategy` → KeywordStrategyPanel) with two tabs (Analysis/Guide). Generation runs as a background job with live progress (ProgressIndicator + TaskPanel), keepalive, 409 concurrent guard, usage-limit refunds, and a post-completion NextStepsCard that scrolls to Quick Wins — this part is genuinely solid. Friction points: (1) Three overlapping generation buttons — "Update changed pages" (incremental), "Full refresh" (local-SEO-first chain, only for local/hybrid), and "Regenerate" (full) — plus a RefreshOrderingPrompt modal; the mental model of which to press is not explained inline, only via hover titles. (2) The Analysis view is ~12 stacked sections (feedback, settings, signals, stats, ranking distribution, quick wins, low-hanging fruit, content gaps, keyword gaps, topic clusters, cannibalization, diff, backlinks, competitive intel, site keywords, opportunities) with no in-page nav/anchors except one scroll target — a long scroll with no progressive disclosure beyond a 'Reference & Analysis' divider. (3) Summary stats are computed from a silently filtered pageMap (VOLUME_THRESHOLD=10, KeywordStrategy.tsx:39,275-291) — 'Impressions'/'Clicks' totals exclude hidden pages so they won't match GSC; only 'Pages Mapped' discloses the filtering. (4) Settings panel still fetches a provider list and contains defaultSeoDataProvider() selection logic (KeywordStrategy.tsx:42-48) but the UI hardcodes a static 'DataForSEO' card — vestigial config. (5) Site Target Keywords volume/KD chips depend on strategy.siteKeywordMetrics which the admin GET no longer returns post table-strip (see issues) — they silently vanish. (6) The /signals endpoint swallows errors and returns 200 + empty array, so the IntelligenceSignals card shows 'No intelligence signals yet' on failure — indistinguishable from truly-empty. (7) Client Keyword Feedback card lists only declines; requested/approved are shown as counts with no list and no link to act on them. (8) No strategy history browser despite 5 retained snapshots — StrategyDiff shows only latest-vs-current. Overall: powerful but dense; it answers 'what should I do next' for content (Draft Brief buttons) better than for the strategy itself.
maps.aiUsage: Generation makes: (a) N parallel page-batch calls (BATCH_SIZE=20, keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:428-433, chunked Promise.all at :981) under named op `keyword-page-assignment` (closed-set candidate selection by stable source-row id, researchMode required, json_object, gpt-5.4-mini); (b) one master site-level call under `keyword-site-synthesis` (3000 maxTokens, Zod safeParse with one schema-feedback retry then typed-empty + deterministic backfill, :1379-1427); (c) `keyword-topic-clusters` during enrichment; legacy `keyword-strategy` op remains for compatibility paths. All ops registered in server/ai-operation-registry.ts:233-360 with explicit contracts. Every call is wrapped in buildSystemPrompt (voice/guardrail layer 2). Grounding is the best in the platform: the prompt receives intelligence slices ['seoContext','insights','learnings'(domain strategy),'clientSignals','contentPipeline'] + localSeo when active (ai-synthesis:296-298); injected blocks include knowledge base, personas, local SEO evidence, client-DECLINED keywords (hard 'do not suggest'), client-REQUESTED keywords (must become content gaps if uncovered), approved keywords, content-gap votes, business priorities, and strategy coverage gaps (strategy keywords without briefs) (:320-360). Raw data grounding: 90-day GSC query-page data, device/country/period comparison, GA4 conversions + events-by-page, DataForSEO domain keywords/keyword gaps/related/question/competitor keywords via buildKeywordUniverse (single pool with branded + declined hard filters, per-workspace provider-credit ceiling). Output passes through a 3-stage sanitizer (post-synthesis, post-enrichment, derived artifacts) that prunes hallucinated keywords not in the pool, plus a deterministic content-gap backfill floor (6) so honesty is preserved (backfilled flag surfaces to the client UI). Weakness: enrichment quality telemetry (GenerationQuality) is computed then only logged (keyword-strategy-generation.ts:588-602), never stored or evaluated; and temperature/model are fixed at gpt-5.4-mini even for the master synthesis, which is the highest-leverage call.
maps.clientUx: Client tab (`/client/:id/strategy` → StrategyTab.tsx) is the most polished client surface audited: narrative health score (calculateStrategyHealth), StrategySnapshot, Recommended Next Steps (3 action cards), Content Opportunities with 'Request brief/post' CTAs that open the pricing modal (source:'strategy') and create real content requests, Page Improvements (quick wins with roiScore), Priority Keywords table with a per-keyword evidence drawer (volume, KD, CPC-based revenue potential — Growth+ TierGated), keyword decline-with-reason modal, keyword request/idea flow, business priorities editor, declined-keywords section with undo, and ?tab= deep-link section expansion (correctly implements the two-halves contract). Feedback genuinely loops back: declines/requests/approvals/votes/priorities all flow into the next generation's prompt. Weak spots: (1) the no-strategy empty state says 'SEO strategy is being prepared … check back soon' (StrategyTab.tsx:318-331) — a false promise if the admin never generates one; there is no admin-side signal that a client is waiting. (2) Client feedback only takes effect at the next admin-triggered regeneration, and nothing tells the client when (or whether) their declined keyword actually left the strategy. (3) backfilled gaps are labeled but the concept ('we padded this list deterministically') is not explained to clients. (4) The pageMap section is gated behind seoClientView visibility config but the endpoint always returns data — visibility is UI-only.
connectionsIn: Keyword Hub → Strategy: REAL. Client/admin tracked keywords enter the keyword universe as candidates (server/keyword-strategy-universe.ts, getTrackedKeywords import; KEYWORD_CANDIDATE_SOURCE), and keyword_feedback (declined/requested/approved) is injected into the synthesis prompt as hard constraints (server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:320-350) and into the keyword evaluation context used by sanitizer + /signals filtering (server/keyword-strategy-context.ts). MCP tools add_keyword_to_strategy / replace_keyword_strategy mutate it directly.

Recommendations → Strategy: MISSING as a direct input. The flow is one-way (strategy feeds recs); no recommendation state (accepted/dismissed recs) is read back during strategy generation. Dismissal patterns would be a cheap negative-signal input.

Action Results (outcome tracking) → Strategy: REAL but thin. The learnings slice is consumed with learningsDomain:'strategy' (server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:297-298, formatForPrompt sections:['learnings']), so workspace learnings/outcome trends reach the prompt. But no per-keyword outcome data (e.g. which past strategy keywords actually moved) is structurally injected.
Content Generation → Strategy: REAL. contentPipeline slice coverageGaps (strategy keywords lacking briefs) are injected into the synthesis prompt to prioritize contentGaps (ai-synthesis ~:358).

Client Dashboard → Strategy: REAL and rich. clientSignals slice carries keyword feedback, content-gap votes, and effectiveBusinessPriorities into the prompt (ai-synthesis:320-356); client business priorities are editable via public-portal routes and flushed into intelligence caches (server/routes/public-portal.ts:431,574).

Insights → Strategy: REAL. insights slice consumed in synthesis; buildStrategyIntelligenceBlock (server/keyword-strategy-helpers.ts:273) formats insight evidence into the master prompt; GET /signals derives momentum/misalignment/content_gap StrategySignals from insights filtered through the keyword evaluation context (server/routes/keyword-strategy.ts:660-688).

External data in: Webflow pages + sitemap discovery (keyword-strategy-pages.ts), GSC 90-day query/page + device/country/period (keyword-strategy-search-data.ts), GA4 conversions/landing/events, DataForSEO via SeoDataProvider (keyword-strategy-seo-data.ts) with quick/full/none credit modes, Local SEO visibility slice + local-strategy-sync staleness signals (local-strategy-sync.ts).

Shares page_keywords and site_keyword_metrics tables; KCC assembles the strategy via assembleStoredKeywordStrategy (server/keyword-command-center.ts:6,423-431); generation seeds/reconciles tracked keywords via reconcileStrategyRankTracking with strategyOwned lifecycle flags (server/keyword-strategy-follow-ons.ts:33-67, rank-tracking-reconciliation.ts); admin chips deep-link 'View in Hub' (src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:873-883, buildHubDeepLinkQuery).

Content Generation: REAL. Admin Content Gaps rows carry 'Draft Brief' / 'Generate Brief' buttons that navigate with fixContext state into content-pipeline/seo-briefs (src/components/strategy/ContentGaps.tsx:76-92); brief/post generation grounds on the strategy through the seoContext slice via buildContentGenerationContext (server/content-brief.ts:785,944,1114); client gap CTAs create paid content requests (StrategyContentOpportunitiesSection.tsx:194-205 → pricing modal source:'strategy').

Strategy-sourced recs map to outcomes downstream (recommendations.ts:208-209 strategy:content-gap → content_published).

maps.intelligenceWiring: Two-directional and unusually complete. CONSUMES: synthesis builds buildWorkspaceIntelligence(ws.id, { slices: ['seoContext','insights','learnings','clientSignals','contentPipeline'] (+'localSeo' when posture active), learningsDomain:'strategy' }) (server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:296-298); the /signals route builds ['seoContext','clientSignals'] (routes/keyword-strategy.ts:666); buildKeywordStrategyUxPayload optionally pulls intelligence for admin diagnostics (keyword-strategy-ux.ts:382, deliberately skipped on the latency-sensitive public read). FEEDS: the strategy is the backbone of the seoContext slice — assembleSeoContext routes through assembleStoredKeywordStrategy for the five normalized arrays + live pageMap (server/intelligence/seo-context-slice.ts:60-105), which then grounds AdminChat, briefs, posts, schema context, and recommendations. Persist invalidates the intelligence cache plus slice:seoContext and slice:pageProfile sub-caches with debounce (keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:205-218). NOT wired: strategy_history snapshots and generationQuality telemetry exist in no slice — the AI cannot reason about strategy evolution over time or generation health.
issues: Admin GET drops siteKeywordMetrics for post-strip workspaces: routes/keyword-strategy.ts destructures only the six arrays from assembleStoredKeywordStrategy (`const { pageMap, contentGaps, quickWins, keywordGaps, topicClusters, cannibalization } = assembled;` ~:253) and serializeKeywordStrategy (:78-113) only spreads the blob, which persistKeywordStrategy now writes with siteKeywordMetrics undefined (keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:101-108). The public route explicitly re-attaches table metrics (routes/public-content.ts:165) but the admin route does not, so the admin 'Site Target Keywords' volume/KD chips (src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:853-862) silently disappear after any new generation. The guard test masks this by seeding the SAME metrics into both the blob and the table (tests/integration/keyword-strategy-admin-assembler.test.ts:41-53), so the assertion at :73 passes via the legacy blob spread.

Strategy generation is outcome-invisible after the first run: recordAction is guarded by `if (!getActionBySource('strategy', ws.id))` (server/keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:184), so only the first-ever generation creates a tracked action. Regenerations — the events whose impact you'd actually want to measure — produce no Action Results entry, breaking the strategy half of the outcome feedback loop.

Admin summary stats are computed on a silently volume-filtered pageMap (VOLUME_THRESHOLD=10, src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:39,275-291): total Impressions/Clicks/CTR exclude hidden pages, so admin numbers won't reconcile with GSC; only the Pages Mapped card discloses the exclusion.

generationQuality telemetry (poolSize, suppressedCount, backfilledCount, floorHit) is computed and logged only (server/keyword-strategy-generation.ts:588-602) — never persisted, surfaced, or fed into evals, so generation-quality regressions are invisible outside log spelunking.

searchSignals (device/country breakdowns, period comparison, GA4 organic overview, top landing pages) is persisted into the blob on every generation (keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:115-121) and passed through the assembler, but has ZERO frontend consumers (grep 'searchSignals' in src returns nothing) — dead weight written to every workspace blob and admin response.

competitorKeywordData (≤150 rows) is persisted to the blob but no UI reads it; CompetitiveIntel re-fetches its own data from /api/seo/competitive-intel with a separate 48h cache (src/components/strategy/CompetitiveIntel.tsx:97-99) — duplicate storage paths for the same intelligence.

Vestigial provider-selection scaffolding in the admin panel: defaultSeoDataProvider()/providerList plumbing (KeywordStrategy.tsx:42-48,112-115) survives while the UI renders a hardcoded static 'DataForSEO' card — the aux providers fetch only powers a boolean now.

Competitors input cannot be cleared: the sync effect refills from workspaceData whenever the field is empty (`if (...competitorDomains?.length && !competitors)`, KeywordStrategy.tsx:154-158), so deleting all competitor domains in the UI immediately repopulates them.

/signals endpoint converts all failures into 200 + empty/unfiltered arrays (routes/keyword-strategy.ts:680-687) — the admin IntelligenceSignals card cannot distinguish 'no signals' from 'signals engine broke', and the EmptyState copy asserts the former.

Client empty state over-promises: 'SEO strategy is being prepared… check back soon' (src/components/client/StrategyTab.tsx:318-331) renders for any workspace where the admin simply never generated a strategy, with no admin-facing signal that a client is waiting on it.
strategy_history retains 5 snapshots (persistence:176-179) but only the latest is ever read (diff route :303, ux builder latestHistory stmt) — 4 of 5 stored snapshots are write-only data.

PATCH /keyword-strategy validates contentGaps/pageMap items with .passthrough() (routes/keyword-strategy.ts:386-407), so arbitrary extra fields flow into the normalized tables' JSON columns unchecked — looser than the generation path's 3-stage sanitizer.

maps.summary: SEO Strategy is the platform's keystone surface: an admin-triggered background job that discovers Webflow pages, pulls GSC/GA4 and DataForSEO data into a single keyword universe, runs closed-set AI assignment (parallel 20-page batches) plus a master site synthesis, sanitizes output against the candidate pool, and persists to a hybrid store (workspace JSON blob for scalars + six normalized tables as truth). The result grounds nearly everything else — recommendations, content briefs, rank tracking, llms.txt, AdminChat, and the client dashboard — via the seoContext intelligence slice and a client-safe public endpoint, with a genuine client feedback loop (declines/requests/votes/priorities) feeding the next generation's prompt. The architecture is mature and heavily tested; the weaknesses are mostly at the edges: an admin read-path regression that drops siteKeywordMetrics, an outcome-tracking loop that only records the first-ever generation, persisted-but-never-rendered data (searchSignals, competitorKeywordData, 4 of 5 history snapshots), and admin UX density with confusing triple-regeneration affordances.
unusedDataOpportunities: searchSignals (device/country mix, 28-day period comparison, GA4 organic overview, top organic landing pages) persisted on every generation but rendered nowhere — a free 'search snapshot' card for admin or client.

strategy_history retains 5 generations but only latest is diffed — could power a strategy-evolution trend (keyword count, coverage %, avg position trajectory) and let the AI reason about whether past strategy changes worked.

generationQuality telemetry (pool size, suppressed, backfilled, floor hits) is log-only — persisting per-generation rows would give a cross-workspace generation-health dashboard and eval fixtures for free.

competitorKeywordData (≤150 rows fetched with paid credits) sits unread in the blob while CompetitiveIntel re-fetches live — reuse it as the cached fallback or a 'competitor keywords you could target' module.

keyword_feedback requested/approved lists are shown to admin only as counts (KeywordStrategy.tsx:422) — surfacing the actual requested keywords with one-click 'add to strategy' would close the client-request loop without a full regeneration.
ga4Conversions / ga4EventsByPage are fetched per generation for the prompt but never persisted — conversion-weighted quick-win ranking is computed once and thrown away.

No 'feedback since last generation' staleness signal: the system knows the strategy generatedAt and every feedback/vote/priority timestamp, but nothing nudges the admin that client signals have accumulated since the last run (the localSync nudge pattern already exists and could be reused).
questionKeywords groups persisted in blob are only used for content-gap FAQ chips — they could pre-seed FAQ sections in brief generation directly.

maps.summary: The Keyword Hub is the admin keyword-lifecycle operating layer at /ws/:id/seo-keywords. A 3,563-line deterministic read-model builder (server/keyword-command-center.ts) merges six sources — stored keyword strategy (siteKeywords/pageMap/contentGaps/keywordGaps), tracked keywords (rank_tracking_config blob + dual-written tracked_keywords row table), daily GSC rank snapshots, keyword_feedback (admin + client), local-SEO candidates/visibility, and lost-visibility discovered queries — into a unified per-keyword row with lifecycle status (in_strategy/tracked/needs_review/raw_evidence/declined/retired), merged metrics, variant rollups, and computed next-actions. Lifecycle mutations (track/pause/retire/decline/restore/add-to-strategy plus a narrow hard-delete channel) write feedback + tracked-keyword state inside transactions, broadcast WS events, log activity, and invalidate the intelligence cache. The whole surface is currently triple-flagged: the new unified KeywordHub shell, the uncapped keyword universe, and value-first scoring are all OFF by default (staging-validation), so production serves the legacy KeywordCommandCenter shell plus a separate Rank Tracker tab. The surface makes zero AI calls itself; it grounds AI elsewhere through keyword_feedback signals and a thin rank-tracking aggregate in the seoContext slice.

maps.intelligenceWiring: Partially wired. CONSUMED: (1) SeoContextSlice carries the keyword strategy/pageMap and a rankTracking aggregate — but only {trackedKeywords count, avgPosition, positionChanges improved/declined/stable} (server/intelligence/seo-context-slice.ts:113-141), formatted as one prompt line (formatters.ts:350-352); (2) ClientSignalsSlice.keywordFeedback (approved/rejected lists + approveRate + topRejectionReasons) via buildKeywordFeedbackSignals (server/keyword-feedback.ts:271); (3) local-seo-slice deliberately reuses the cheap candidate builder (local-seo-slice.ts:60-82). Mutations correctly call invalidateIntelligenceCache and broadcast INTELLIGENCE_SIGNALS_UPDATED. NOT WIRED: the Hub's actual read model — lifecycle states (needs_review/retired/declined counts), Layer-1 value scores, dollar values, the raw-evidence universe, GSC variant rollups, and per-keyword rank history are in NO slice. AdminChat/AI cannot answer "which keywords are retired/need review" or "what's our top keyword opportunity by value" despite the server computing exactly that for the UI. This is a partial Data Flow Rule 6 violation: the data exists in tables KCC reads, but the assembled lifecycle view is UI-only.
connectionsOut: SEO Strategy — REAL, strong: keyword_feedback written by Hub actions is consumed by strategy generation (server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:325 declined hard-excluded; :338 requested prioritized; :344-346 approved injected as 'APPROVED KEYWORDS' prompt section) and keyword-strategy-ux.ts; ADD_TO_STRATEGY/DECLINE/RESTORE broadcast STRATEGY_UPDATED + INTELLIGENCE_SIGNALS_UPDATED (server/keyword-command-center.ts broadcastKeywordCommandCenterAction). CAVEAT: ADD_TO_STRATEGY does NOT write the strategy artifact — inclusion in keywordStrategy.siteKeywords waits for the next AI regeneration honoring a prompt hint, while the Hub immediately labels the row IN_STRATEGY (lifecycleStatus, keyword-command-center.ts:567).

Recommendations — REAL, indirect: getDeclinedKeywords/getRequestedKeywords consumed by server/keyword-recommendations.ts:15 and server/recommendations.ts:24 (declined content-gap candidates skipped at recommendations.ts:1385); Layer-1 keyword value score feeds the Layer-2 opportunity-value spine for keyword-bearing content-gap recs via server/keyword-strategy-enrichment.ts; deriveValueIntent is the single shared intent classifier across both layers. No Hub UI affordance creates a recommendation directly.

Action Results (outcome tracking) — MISSING: no Hub lifecycle action creates a tracked_action; outcome-measurement.ts measures via getPageTrend (GSC page-level) + page_keywords, never via the Hub's per-keyword rank_snapshots; tracked-keyword baselines (baselinePosition/baselineClicks/baselineImpressions) are written but consumed only by rank-tracking modules. 'Did tracking/promoting this keyword pay off?' is unanswerable.

Content Generation — REAL but thin: the 'generate_brief' next-action navigates to content-pipeline with fixContext {primaryKeyword, pageType} (src/components/KeywordHub.tsx:323-334; ContentPipeline.tsx:99 receiver) — a navigation prefill only, no persisted link; keyword strategy/pageMap reach brief/post generation via the seoContext slice, not via Hub lifecycle state; content-pipeline-slice computes strategy-keywords-without-briefs independently (server/intelligence/content-pipeline-slice.ts:74-84).

Client Dashboard — REAL, indirect: tracked keywords (including Hub-promoted ones) render in the client Strategy tab (useStrategyTrackedKeywords) and stay live via RANK_TRACKING_UPDATED/STRATEGY_UPDATED broadcasts that Hub mutations emit; declined keywords render in StrategyDeclinedKeywordsSection. No client-facing Hub equivalent exists (by design — admin evidence labels are kept out of client copy).
connectionsIn: SEO Strategy — REAL, primary row source: assembleStoredKeywordStrategy + resolveSiteKeywordMetrics + listPageKeywordsLite supply siteKeywords/metrics/pageMap/contentGaps/keywordGaps rows and the strategy-ux explanations; KeywordStrategy.tsx:875 deep-links a keyword into the Hub (?q=) and :414 opens the Hub directly.

Recommendations — WEAK: PROMOTE_EVIDENCE stamps source=TRACKED_KEYWORD_SOURCE.RECOMMENDATION on the tracked row, and gap-provenance (sourceGapKey) flows in via keyword feedback approval, but the Recommendations surface has no deep-link or handoff INTO the Hub.

maps.adminUx: Two parallel shells with materially different quality. PRODUCTION (all three flags OFF): the legacy KeywordCommandCenter — a single row of 18 filter chips with no grouping, whose counts are computed from the visible 50-row page until the deliberately deferred (150ms + fetch) summary arrives (KeywordCommandCenter.tsx:186-218) — i.e. misleading badge numbers on first paint; no column sorting (rowsQuery omits sort → server 'priority' default); rank measurement lives in a separate Rank Tracker tab. STAGING (flag ON): the Hub is genuinely better — 6 segments with real counts + an advanced-filter menu, opportunity-first default sort, an honest metric-window label and truncation banner, a strong per-keyword journey drawer (origin → tracking → rank sparkline → per-market local visibility → lifecycle), bulk actions with protected-selection confirmation, deep links, focus-trap a11y, and proper loading/empty/error states with a clear-filters CTA. Real problems: (1) protected keywords (pinned/client-requested/strategy-owned) can be retired/declined in ONE click from the row menu or drawer because the Hub silently passes force:true whenever disabledReason is set (KeywordActionMenu.tsx:90, KeywordHub.tsx:346-351) — the legacy shell's ConfirmDialog step was dropped, defeating the documented protection contract; (2) with the flag ON, seo-ranks redirects into the Hub (App.tsx:450) but the Hub has NO manual add-keyword input — the only one lives in the now-unreachable RankTracker.tsx:402-408, so a keyword with zero GSC/strategy evidence cannot be added from the admin UI at all; (3) applying an advanced filter doesn't reset pagination (useKeywordHubState.ts:174-179), desyncing client page state from the server-clamped page and making Previous/Next behave erratically; (4) the answer to "what should I do next?" is decent at row level (next-actions) but there is no triage queue ordering — needs_review is a segment, not a workflow. Click economy is good: list → drawer → action is 2-3 clicks.
maps.clientUx: No client Keyword Hub surface — deliberate per docs/rules/keyword-command-center.md ("Client Strategy remains client-safe"). Clients interact with keywords only through the Strategy tab: they see strategy keywords and tracked keywords (useStrategyTrackedKeywords, live via WS), can add/remove tracked keywords, and approve/decline/request keywords via /api/public/keyword-feedback — all of which flow correctly into the Hub as protected CLIENT_REQUESTED rows and REQUESTED-filter items. The boundary is sound. Gaps: clients who request a keyword get no visible progress loop ("you asked for X — here is its rank trend since we started tracking it"); declined-keyword lists shown to clients render the normalized (lowercased/canonicalized) keyword string because the Hub's upsertFeedback stores keywordComparisonKey output as the keyword (keyword-command-center.ts:3145-3156), slightly degrading client-facing copy. The absence of a client surface is not itself a gap, but the missing rank-progress feedback for client-requested keywords is a missed engagement/retention hook.
maps.aiUsage: The Hub surface itself makes ZERO AI calls — the read model, value scoring (Layer-1 computeKeywordValueScore), explanations (keyword-strategy-ux.ts), and next-actions are fully deterministic; research_keywords is a paid SEO-provider call (with recordPaidCall budget tracking and research handles), not an AI call. The Hub's AI role is as a GROUNDING SOURCE for other surfaces: (1) keyword_feedback written here shapes the strategy-generation prompt — declined keywords hard-filtered, requested prioritized, approved injected as an 'APPROVED KEYWORDS — treat as safe strategic direction' block (keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:325-346); (2) ClientSignalsSlice.keywordFeedback (approved/rejected + approveRate + rejection reasons) reaches prompts via formatters.ts:719; (3) SeoContextSlice.rankTracking gives AI one aggregate line (count/avgPosition/changes, formatters.ts:350-352). Grounding quality is therefore SHALLOW relative to what the surface knows: AI consumers never see per-keyword lifecycle state, value scores, dollar values, variant rollups, raw-evidence volume, or rank history — the richest keyword dataset in the platform is mostly invisible to AdminChat, brief generation, and recommendation context builders beyond the strategy artifact and feedback lists.
issues: Hub single-row protected-action bypass: KeywordActionMenu.tsx:90 and KeywordHub.tsx:346-351 auto-pass force:true whenever a lifecycle action carries disabledReason, so a pinned/client-requested/strategy-owned keyword can be retired/declined/paused in one click with no confirmation — regressing the legacy shell's requiresProtectedConfirmation → ConfirmDialog flow (KeywordCommandCenter.tsx handleAction) and violating docs/rules/keyword-command-center.md's 'must require explicit confirmation/force semantics'. The bulk path kept its confirm dialog; the single-row and drawer paths lost it.

No manual add-keyword affordance when keyword-hub flag is ON: src/App.tsx:450 redirects seo-ranks → seo-keywords, making RankTracker.tsx:402-408 (the only 'Add keyword to track...' input) unreachable; the Hub shell has only a search box. A net-new keyword with no GSC/strategy/local evidence cannot enter tracking via the admin UI.

useKeywordHubState.ts:174-179 setAdvancedFilter omits the page-reset that setSegment and search apply; server clamps page (keyword-command-center.ts paginateRows:989) so hub.page desyncs from pageInfo.page, and HubKeywordList's Previous/Next compute from the stale hub.page prop.

ADD_TO_STRATEGY semantic gap: the action writes feedback='approved' + tracked source=STRATEGY_SITE_KEYWORD but never touches keywordStrategy.siteKeywords/pageMap; the Hub immediately shows IN_STRATEGY (lifecycleStatus, keyword-command-center.ts:567) while the Strategy surface and client strategy view exclude the keyword until an AI regeneration happens to honor the 'APPROVED KEYWORDS' prompt hint (keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:344-346) — a non-guaranteed, cross-surface inconsistency the success message ('added to the strategy operating loop') papers over.

upsertFeedback stores the normalized comparison key as the keyword display string (keyword-command-center.ts:3145-3156), violating the doc rule 'preserve raw display strings for user-facing keyword labels'; client-facing declined-keyword sections render canonicalized text.

Keyword lifecycle/value data absent from intelligence slices (Data Flow Rule 6 partial violation): seo-context-slice.ts:113-141 exposes only count/avgPosition/changes; no slice carries needs_review/retired/declined counts, Layer-1 value scores, or top opportunities — AdminChat is blind to the Hub's core state despite intelligence-cache invalidation being wired.

Hub ↔ Action Results loop completely missing: TrackedKeyword baselines (baselinePosition/baselineClicks/baselineImpressions) are written but consumed by no outcome module; outcome-measurement.ts:16,30 measures via GSC page trends + page_keywords only; no tracked_action is created when a keyword is promoted/tracked, so keyword-level decisions are never scored.

Triple-stacked OFF-by-default flags (shared/types/feature-flags.ts:37,44,49) mean production runs the legacy shell while two near-duplicate shells (KeywordHub.tsx 511 lines + KeywordCommandCenter.tsx 663 lines) and RankTracker are all maintained; the value-scoring 'opportunity' default sort in the Hub degrades to crude volume/difficulty computeOpportunityScore until the second flag flips — drift risk until the P5 cutover deletes the legacy paths.

Legacy KCC filter chips render placeholder counts derived from the current 50-row page until the deferred summary loads (KeywordCommandCenter.tsx:186-218) — factually wrong badge numbers during the first ~second of every visit on the production surface.

No 'brief exists' indicator on content_gap rows: briefs store targetKeyword and content-pipeline-slice already computes strategy-keywords-without-briefs, but the Hub row/drawer never shows it, so 'Generate brief' can silently duplicate work.

Every rows/summary/detail request rebuilds the read model from scratch (no server-side caching even on the skinny path; buildKeywordCommandCenterRowsSkinny logs totalMs/heap per request) — each pagination/sort click re-runs candidate selection + finalize across all sources; acceptable today, a scaling cliff with keyword-universe-full ON (2,000-row safety ceiling × per-row finalize).
unusedDataOpportunities: Per-keyword rank history (rank_snapshots) — used only for the drawer sparkline; not consumed by outcome measurement, brief-refresh decisions, or client progress reporting on requested keywords.

Tracked-keyword baselines (baselinePosition/baselineClicks/baselineImpressions) — captured at track-time precisely so 'did this pay off' can be answered, but no consumer outside rank-tracking modules exists; a cheap keyword-level outcome bridge into tracked_actions is sitting unwired.

Layer-1 value components + keywordDollarValue ($/mo estimates from server/scoring/keyword-value-money.ts) — computed for the drawer's reasons list only; not surfaced in client briefing ROI narratives, recommendation explainability, or the client strategy view.

GSC variant rollups (variants aggregated onto parent rows with summed clicks/impressions and MIN position) — rich phrasing data ideal for brief secondary keywords/FAQ generation, exposed only as a sub-row toggle in the admin list.
keyword_metrics_cache trend arrays (12-point volume trend cached per keyword globally) — never rendered or used; seasonality signals for content timing are free and unused.

Feedback patterns (approveRate, topRejectionReasons from buildKeywordFeedbackSignals) — computed into ClientSignalsSlice but only the raw approved/rejected lists reach prompts; rejection-reason patterns could steer strategy generation away from whole keyword classes.

Lost-visibility queries (detectLostVisibility runs daily) — exposed only as a passive Hub filter; no automated insight/recommendation generated when a previously-ranking query disappears, which is exactly the 'so what' event a client cares about.
Client-requested keyword progress — the CLIENT_REQUESTED source + rank history together enable a 'you asked, here's the trend' client card; nothing renders it.

maps.summary: The Recommendations surface is a fully deterministic (no-AI) rule engine (server/recommendations.ts, 2,384 lines) that fans in ~12 data sources — audit snapshots, GSC/GA4 traffic, keyword strategy tables (quick wins, content gaps, page keywords, keyword gaps, topic clusters, cannibalization), analytics insights (ctr_opportunity, freshness_alert, conversion_attribution), content decay, diagnostics, and local-SEO visibility — and mints a single per-workspace RecommendationSet JSON blob, scored by a unified Opportunity Value (OV) model (EMV/ROI-based, 0-100) with per-workspace calibration from measured outcomes. It is regenerated post-audit, on debounced opportunity events, after keyword-strategy follow-ons, and after outcome measurement; status changes flow through a state machine, completions are auto-resolved in-place when SEO writes touch affected pages, and completed recs feed the outcome-tracking loop (predictedEmv snapshot → baseline capture → scored outcomes → win-rate learnings that re-rank future recs). Client UX (InsightsEngine in the Health tab + the #1-priority card on Overview) is the primary surface; the admin gets only a compact embed, chat context, and meeting-brief/divergence diagnostics.

maps.adminUx: Thin and fragmented. The admin's only list view is the compact InsightsEngine embed on WorkspaceHome (top 4 fix_now/fix_soon with a 'Fix' nav button) — with tier hardcoded to "premium" (WorkspaceHome.tsx:628) and a footer reading "view in Site Health", which is the CLIENT tab name; the admin has no full recommendation management surface at all (full mode renders only inside ClientDashboard). To see the complete queue the admin must open the client portal or ask AdminChat. There is no admin control over status (the PATCH route is client-portal-authed; admins acting through the global gate can hit it, but no admin component does), no way to pin/reorder/suppress a rec for a client, no view of dismissed recs (so un-dismiss, which the state machine allows, is unreachable), and no per-rec OV breakdown anywhere in admin UI — the OV diagnostics that exist (OvDivergencePanel) show the historical shadow log buried inside MeetingBriefPage. Regen is reachable via the client-facing Refresh button or the jobs route; activity log records only explicit generations, and client complete/dismiss actions are never logged to the activity feed, so the admin cannot see clients triaging their plan.
maps.clientUx: The strongest client surface in the platform, with real care: priority-grouped accordion (fix_now expanded by default), contextual loading/empty/error states wired to the background job (job message/error shown in the empty state), per-rec insight copy, affected-page chips, traffic-at-risk, non-dollarized gain strings, purchase CTAs that drop into a cart, premium upsell footer, and a reconciled #1-priority card on Overview with "why this is #1" OV component bars. It mostly answers "what should I do next?". Weaknesses: (1) the header claims items are "ranked by Opportunity Value" but the list never shows the OV value or breakdown — only the legacy high/medium/low impact + effort badges, so ordering within a tier looks arbitrary; the OV breakdown exists only on the Overview #1 card, labeled "ROI 62" (a 0-100 normalized score masquerading as ROI); (2) content_creation recs dead-end — no productType means the only CTA is "I'll Handle This", with no bridge to request a brief from the studio; (3) Refresh failures are swallowed silently (InsightsEngine.tsx:169); (4) dismiss is one-way in the UI — no dismissed view, no undo; (5) completed recs whose issue resurfaces silently flip back to pending with no "this came back" framing (the auto-resolve insight prefix exists only for the resolved direction).
maps.aiUsage: Recommendation generation itself makes ZERO AI calls — every title/description/insight is a deterministic template (signal-story-registry.ts + inline copy), and scoring is the pure computeOpportunityValue model. This is a deliberate strength (cheap, reproducible, no hallucination) but means rec copy never reflects brand voice or client context. buildRecommendationGenerationContext is used purely as a typed data fetch (backlinkProfile, effectiveBusinessPriorities, learnings) with slices ['seoContext','clientSignals','learnings'], tokenBudget 800 — yet the builder still runs formatForPrompt and the resulting promptContext is discarded (recommendations.ts:1085 never reads it): wasted work each regen. Recommendation DATA is well-grounded into downstream AI: AdminChat gets active recs verbatim (admin-chat-context.ts:841), all slice-based AI consumers get the queue counts (operational slice) and the #1 rec's OV components with admin-only emvPerWeek (seoContext.topOpportunity, formatted at formatters.ts:424), meeting-brief AI gets the top rec, and the monthly digest AI gets learnings/insights via the same builder. Grounding quality is good; the main gap is that AI consumers see only the #1 rec's OV detail, not the top-N, and the client advisor cannot explain items 2-10.
issues: Outcome-calibration mislabeling: outcome-backfill.ts:196 hardcodes actionType 'audit_fix_applied' for EVERY completed rec it backfills, contradicting the compile-time-exhaustive recommendationOutcomeActionType mapping (server/recommendations.ts:198-242) whose stated purpose is honest winRateByActionType calibration. Since recs completed via resolveRecommendationsForChange, auto-resolve, and work-order/approval applies never call recordAction with sourceType 'recommendation', the recurring backfill cron is the path that creates their actions — so content/keyword_gap/topic_cluster/local rec outcomes are systematically logged as audit_fix_applied, polluting the exact win-rate signal that multiplies future rank scores (outcome-learning-default-path.ts:54)

predictedEmv pairing leaks: only manual client PATCH completions snapshot predictedEmv (routes/recommendations.ts:216); platform-executed completions (auto-resolve at recommendations.ts:2295, in-place resolve at :463) get backfilled with predictedEmv: null (outcome-backfill.ts:209), so the P6 realized-vs-predicted calibration dataset accrues mostly from the rarest completion path

Dead component: src/components/client/FixRecommendations.tsx is referenced nowhere (zero imports across src/ and server/) yet was recently updated with P4 contract comments — misleading maintenance surface

Pending-rec ID churn: the merge (recommendations.ts:2264-2281) preserves ids only for in_progress/completed/dismissed recs; pending recs get fresh random ids every regen, so external references to pending recs (briefing candidates built from status==='pending' recs at briefing-candidates.ts:159, frontend expanded-state Sets) dangle across regens

No activity logging for client rec actions: routes/recommendations.ts never calls addActivity on complete/dismiss — violates Data Flow rule 4 and leaves the admin blind to client triage; generation itself logs only on reason==='explicit' (recommendation-generation-job.ts:30)

maps.summary: Action Results tracks platform actions (rec completions, content publishes, insight resolutions, approval applies, briefs, strategy gens, voice calibration, decay/internal-link suggestions) as `tracked_actions` rows with a GSC baseline, then a daily cron scores each at 7/30/60/90-day checkpoints against current 14-day GSC averages using per-action-type metrics/thresholds, producing win/loss/neutral outcomes plus an attributed dollar value (clicks delta × page CPC). Scored outcomes aggregate into per-workspace `workspace_learnings` (daily) and `action_playbooks` (weekly) that feed the intelligence LearningsSlice, recommendation/keyword score multipliers (`buildOutcomeAdjustment`), OV calibration, and AI prompt blocks. Admin sees a 5-tab "Action Results" dashboard per workspace plus a cross-workspace overview; clients see a wins ledger (WinsSurface) and a "we called it" card via intelligence. The architecture is genuinely closed-loop on paper, but scoring for ~6 of 15 action types is structurally broken (non-existent primary metrics fabricate neutral/loss scores), unimplemented suggestions are scored as if executed, and significant chunks of the built UI (per-action detail, notes, client scorecard) are dead code.

maps.adminUx: The dashboard (Sidebar 'Action Results', default Top Wins tab) reads well at a glance — scorecard ring, win-rate bars, skeletons and action-oriented empty states everywhere. But it dead-ends one level down: (1) The Actions feed lets you FILTER by score, yet rows never display the score, checkpoint, or measured delta — the expanded row shows only attribution/window/baseline. The per-action endpoint that returns outcomes (GET /actions/:actionId) and the add-note mutation are fully implemented server-side and hook-side but no component uses them (useOutcomeAction/useAddOutcomeNote: zero consumers), so an admin literally cannot answer "what happened to this specific action and why?" from the UI. (2) The feed's delta column is permanently blank because trailingHistory is never populated by any recordAction caller. (3) The action-type filter dropdown omits the 5 newer types (competitor_gap_closed, cluster_published, cannibalization_resolved, local_*) that the label map knows about. (4) Nothing in the UI distinguishes not_acted_on suggestion rows from executed actions except the buried expanded-row attribution text, so the scorecard silently mixes "things we did" with "things we suggested". (5) Top Wins shows % deltas but never the attributed dollar value the system computes — the most persuasive number in the schema is invisible. (6) earlySignal (7-day on-track/no-movement) is never shown, so "pending measurement" is a black box for 30 days. (7) The diagnostics endpoint (anomaly detection for empty baselines, relative URLs, overdue scoring) has no UI at all. Overall: good summary surfaces, no drill-down, weak "what should I do next" — playbooks display patterns but nothing actionable links them to creating the next action.
maps.clientUx: Two live surfaces: WinsSurface ("What we shipped") on the client briefing page — clean tier-gated win rows with plain-language action labels, hides itself entirely when empty (good) — and PredictionShowcaseCard on OverviewTab from intelligence weCalledIt. But the flagship client artifacts were never shipped: OutcomeSummary.tsx (a complete tiered scorecard with free/growth/premium views, trend narration, category breakdown) and WeCalledIt.tsx (month-grouped wins with TierGates) are imported nowhere, and their public /summary endpoint has no live consumer — substantial finished work delivering zero client value. In what IS live: WinsSurface's "See full history →" is a dead href="#" ("Coming soon"); the free-tier teaser says "X wins shipped this month" but counts the all-time top-10 wins; the win label comes from actionType while the server's OutcomeWinEntry.recommendation field is a fabricated "<action_type> action" string — the "we recommended X" promise is not backed by the actual recommendation text. Clients also never see dollar attribution despite it existing per outcome (only the monthly digest uses it). Net: the proof-of-value loop — the whole commercial point of outcome tracking — is materially under-delivered to clients.
maps.aiUsage: This surface makes NO direct AI calls — measurement, scoring, learnings, and playbook detection are fully deterministic (a deliberate and sound choice). Its AI role is as a context PRODUCER: (1) formatLearningsSection renders the LearningsSlice into the shared intelligence prompt block consumed by AdminChat (dedicated 'learnings' section), content brief generation, recommendation generation, and keyword strategy synthesis via buildContentGenerationContext/buildRecommendationGenerationContext; (2) buildOutcomeLearningStatusNote injects explicit availability disclaimers (no_data/degraded) into brief, decay-refresh, keyword-rec, and strategy prompts so models don't hallucinate prior wins — a good grounding pattern enforced by docs/rules/outcome-learning-default-path.md. Grounding quality concern: the learnings text fed to AI inherits the upstream scoring defects — fabricated neutral/loss scores for broken metrics, suggestion rows scored as executed actions, and position-proxied "difficulty" win rates — so prompts can assert e.g. "content refreshes recover traffic 0% of the time" or misleading difficulty sweet spots with stated confidence. The deterministic multipliers (buildOutcomeAdjustment, ov-calibration) are clamped (0.75–1.25) which bounds the damage but also bounds the learning.
issues: SCORING BROKEN for ~6/15 action types — primary metrics that don't exist in any snapshot: content_refreshed→'click_recovery', internal_link_added→'target_improvement', brief_created→'content_produced' (outcome-scoring-defaults.ts:27-42); computeDelta (outcome-measurement.ts:188-198) reads them as 0 vs 0 → every checkpoint scores 'neutral'. A client-completed content-refresh recommendation can NEVER score a win. Worse: audit_fix_applied→'page_health_score' and voice_calibrated→'voice_score' exist in baseline but fetchCurrentMetrics only returns GSC fields, so a baseline with page_health_score=70 vs current 0 computes −100% → systematic 'loss'. The SEARCH_METRICS guards (outcome-measurement.ts:341-448) deliberately skip non-search metrics, so nothing catches this.

Unimplemented suggestions are scored as executed actions: getPendingMeasurement (outcome-tracking.ts:58) and computeWorkspaceLearnings (workspace-learnings.ts:392-409) never filter attribution='not_acted_on', so decay suggestions (routes/content-decay.ts:115) and internal-link suggestions (routes/webflow-analysis.ts:260) receive win/loss scores that pollute win rates, learnings, the client scorecard, and the rec-ranking multipliers regardless of whether anything was done.

Dead client components: src/components/client/OutcomeSummary.tsx and WeCalledIt.tsx have zero importers; GET /api/public/outcomes/:id/summary and useClientOutcomeSummary have no live consumer (only wsInvalidation still references the query key).

Per-action drill-down never built: useOutcomeAction + useAddOutcomeNote (src/hooks/admin/useOutcomes.ts:34,93) have zero component consumers; GET /api/outcomes/:ws/actions/:id and POST .../note are UI-dead; OutcomeActionFeed offers a score filter but never renders scores/outcomes on rows.

trailingHistory is never populated by ANY recordAction caller (always the empty default, outcome-tracking.ts:209) — OutcomeActionFeed.tsx:54-69 delta display is permanently null; dead schema concept carried through types, Zod, mappers, and archive.

Learnings semantics are misleading: winRateByDifficultyRange bins baseline GSC *position* (workspace-learnings.ts:216-226) but buildOutcomeAdjustment matches real provider keyword difficulty against those bins (outcome-learning-default-path.ts:65-79) — the difficulty multiplier is calibrated on incommensurate data; winRateByFormat keys are sourceTypes ('post','insight'), not content formats; keywordVolumeSweetSpot is just min/max impressions of wins; optimalWordCount is a permanent null placeholder.

strategy_keyword_added is recorded once per workspace EVER (guard !getActionBySource('strategy', ws.id), keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:184) with pageUrl=null + position metric → permanently inconclusive; the action type meant to learn strategy effectiveness collects ~no usable data.

measurementWindow is accepted via API (7–365, routes/outcomes.ts:257) and stored, but measurement ignores it — checkpoints fixed at 7/30/60/90 and completion always at 90 (outcome-measurement.ts:59, outcome-tracking.ts:378); only the un-consumed diagnostics endpoint reads it.

suggestPlaybook (outcome-playbooks.ts:123) has zero consumers — playbooks never drive anything; detection itself is crude: win = ANY action on the page won (confounded credit), avgDaysToResult hardcoded 0 (outcome-playbooks.ts:109).

N+1 query storm: /api/outcomes/overview (routes/outcomes.ts:145-193) runs per workspace computeScorecard (1+N queries), getTopWinsForWorkspace (re-fetches actions + up to 50 outcome queries), AND a third full actions+outcomes loop for scoredLast30d — ~3×W×A queries per render of the global overview.

LearningsSlice availability 'disabled' is a dead union value — assembleLearnings never produces it, so the 'disabled' branch of buildOutcomeLearningStatusNote (outcome-learning-default-path.ts:100) can never execute; there is no actual per-workspace disable switch despite the contract docs implying one.

Client 'We called it' framing isn't backed by data: OutcomeWinEntry.recommendation is fabricated as '<action_type> action' (routes/outcomes.ts:395) — no link back to the original recommendation title; WinsSurface dead 'See full history' link (WinsSurface.tsx:135-144) and free-tier teaser miscounts all-time wins as 'this month'.

getWorkspaceCounts labels measurement_complete=1 as 'scored' (outcome-tracking.ts:116-122) — completion ≠ scored (all-inconclusive actions count as scored); feeds overview attention logic and diagnostics tableCounts.

OutcomeActionFeed type-filter dropdown (OutcomeActionFeed.tsx:12-24) omits the 5 newer action types that ACTION_TYPE_LABELS already defines — competitor_gap_closed/cluster_published/cannibalization_resolved/local_* actions can't be filtered to.

Backfill module claims to launch 'with months of learnings' (outcome-backfill.ts:2-3) but creates captured_at-only baselines that score permanently inconclusive for search metrics — it mostly generates noise rows; the cron comment (outcome-crons.ts:199) admits it is only a recovery net.

Multi-action attribution confounding unaddressed: relatedActions are tagged into context (outcome-measurement.ts:454-466) but every overlapping action on a page still receives full independent credit for the same GSC delta — double-counted wins/losses in learnings and dollar attribution.
unusedDataOpportunities: predicted_emv accrues on every rec-completion action (P4) but nothing consumes it — the realized-vs-predicted EMV calibration loop (P6) is unimplemented; ov-calibration still uses only score weights. The data needed to answer 'are our dollar projections honest?' is being collected and ignored.

attributed_value (real dollars per outcome) is invisible in the entire admin UI — Top Wins shows percentages only; only the monthly digest surfaces it. A 'value generated this quarter' number per workspace is one query away (getWinsWithValueByWorkspace already exists).

earlySignal (7-day on_track/no_movement/too_early) is captured for every action and broadcast in OUTCOME_SCORED but rendered nowhere — pending actions could show 'early signal: on track' instead of a 30-day black box, for both admin and client.
seasonalTag (month/quarter) is stamped on every action (outcome-tracking.ts:195-198) but no learnings dimension uses it — seasonal win-rate patterns are computable today.

competitorContext/CompetitorMovement types, Zod schemas, and DB column exist but NOTHING populates them (only schemas/outcome-schemas.ts references) — the 'did a competitor move explain this outcome?' confound is structurally planned and entirely unbuilt, despite SEMRush/DataForSEO access elsewhere in the platform.

Diagnostics endpoint (routes/outcomes.ts:419) computes empty-baseline/relative-URL/overdue/orphan anomalies that would have caught several of the scoring bugs above — surfacing it on the admin dashboard (or in platform-health checks) is nearly free.

action playbooks could feed recommendation generation ('this 2-step sequence wins 80% of the time on this site — suggest step 2 after step 1 completes') via the already-existing suggestPlaybook; currently display-only.
Archive tables hold ≥24-month history with no read path — long-horizon 'value delivered since onboarding' narratives for client retention are stored but unreachable.

context.notes + the note endpoint exist server-side; wiring the already-written useAddOutcomeNote into an action detail view would let admins record why an outcome happened, enriching learnings with qualitative labels.

maps.summary: Content Generation spans three sub-pipelines: (1) content briefs — a background job (content-brief-generation-job.ts) that assembles GSC queries, GA4 page performance, SEO-provider keyword metrics, scraped SERP/reference/style-example pages, intelligence slices (seoContext, insights, learnings, eeatAssets, pageProfile) and page-type contracts into a single GPT-5.4 JSON call persisted to content_briefs; (2) posts — a section-by-section Claude-sonnet-4-6 (GPT-5.4 fallback) generation job (intro → N sections → conclusion → unification pass → SEO meta) with version history, an admin review checklist whose provenance-sensitive items are force-failed for human verification against a token-overlap evidence ledger, AI fixes, and Webflow CMS publish (manual route + fire-and-forget auto-publish on approval); (3) the copy pipeline for website page copy (blueprint entries → copy_sections with steering notes and copy-intelligence patterns). Clients interact through content requests (request → brief_generated → client_review → approved → in_progress → post_review → delivered/published) with inline TipTap editing, approve/request-changes, and email notifications. Outcome actions (brief_created, content_published + GSC baselines) feed the Action Results surface, and outcome learnings flow back into generation prompts via the learnings slice.

maps.intelligenceWiring: Feeds: ContentPipelineSlice (server/intelligence/content-pipeline-slice.ts) summarizes briefs/posts/requests/matrices/workOrders/seoEdits counts, coverage gaps (strategy keywords with no brief), decay alerts, matrix cannibalization warnings, suggested-brief count, copy pipeline approval rates, rewritePlaybook and contentPricing — consumed by AdminChat, MCP get_workspace_intelligence, and the ContentPipeline UI cannibalization alert. Every mutation calls invalidateContentPipelineIntelligence(). Consumes: brief generation uses buildContentGenerationContext with slices [seoContext+eeatAssets], [pageProfile], [insights], [learnings(content)] in four separate calls (content-brief.ts:1114/1143/1314/1331); post generation uses only buildSeoPromptContext → [seoContext, learnings] (content-posts-ai.ts:148) — insights/clientSignals/eeatAssets/contentPipeline are NOT in the post-writing context (brief is assumed to carry them). Copy pipeline uses buildSeoPromptBlocks + buildWorkspaceIntelligence directly. NOT wired into any slice: voiceScore/voiceFeedback distribution, AI-review outcomes (not even persisted), post version-edit patterns, client steering feedback text, generation-style outcomes.
connectionsOut: Action Results (real): recordAction('brief_created') at brief generation (content-brief-generation-job.ts:176) and recordAction('content_published') + captureBaselineFromGsc on publish — both the manual route (routes/content-publish.ts:179-193) and auto-publish path (routes/content-posts.ts:411-425), deduped via getActionByWorkspaceAndSource. This is the surface's strongest loop: published posts get GSC baselines so outcomes can be scored.

SEO Strategy / Keyword Hub (real, partial): queueKeywordStrategyPostUpdateFollowOns({workspaceId}) after manual publish triggers recommendation/strategy regen (routes/content-publish.ts:224) — but the auto-publish-on-approval path in routes/content-posts.ts does NOT call it, so approval-published posts never refresh strategy. Coverage gaps (strategy keywords lacking briefs) are computed in the contentPipeline slice and surfaced to AdminChat.

Recommendations (weak/missing): contentPipeline IS in buildRecommendationGenerationContext's default slices, but the actual recommendation generator overrides slices to ['seoContext','clientSignals','learnings'] (server/recommendations.ts:1091), so recommendation generation cannot see in-flight briefs/posts — it can recommend content that is already being produced. Only content_gaps/decay direct reads provide partial dedup.

connectionsIn: SEO Strategy (real): brief generation matches targetKeyword against the live pageMap from the seoContext slice (content-brief.ts:1137) and injects page-level optimization issues via the pageProfile slice; keywordStrategy.businessContext is the default business context; content gaps and strategy cards create content_requests with source='strategy' and StrategyCardContext threaded into the brief prompt (content-brief-generation-job.ts:256); SERP-feature directives come from page_keywords.serpFeatures.

Recommendations / Insights (real): insights slice injects cannibalization warnings, content-decay, ranking-opportunity quick wins, and page-health into the brief prompt (buildBriefIntelligenceBlockFromSlice, content-brief.ts:749-771); buildPipelineSignals(insights) powers the AiSuggested briefs panel (routes/content-briefs.ts:102-111); content decay produces decayQueryContext for refresh briefs; PageIntelligence navigates with fixContext to pre-fill the brief generator.

maps.adminUx: Structurally good but operationally noisy. The pipeline shell (Planner → Briefs → Posts → Subscriptions with a WorkflowStepper, decay alert, cannibalization alert, AI-suggested briefs) gives a clear 'what next'. Brief and post generation are properly background-jobbed with TaskPanel progress and per-section status persisted so partial results survive failures. Weaknesses: (1) the Briefs tab is a mega-surface — one ContentBriefs component hosts the post editor, generated-posts list, client request list, generator form, and brief list with ~25 useState hooks and tangled fixContext ref choreography; posts also appear in a second, slightly different list under the Posts tab (ContentManager), so the same post has two homes. (2) Many mutation failures are swallowed: saveBriefField, handleRegenerateBrief, handleUpdateRequestStatus, PostEditor.saveField all catch-and-console.error with no toast — an admin can edit a brief field, get a 400, and see nothing. (3) The review checklist gates draft→review on all 6 items (good), and provenance items honestly refuse AI auto-pass, but the AI review result is never persisted — closing the editor loses it, and re-running costs tokens. (4) Approving a post can trigger a silent fire-and-forget auto-publish whose failure is only a log line; the admin sees 'Approved' and has no idea the Webflow publish failed. (5) The ai-fix flow has a diff modal (good), but factual fixes always target section 0 regardless of where the flagged claim lives. (6) Voice scoring is a manual button, never run automatically, so it's effectively unused signal.
maps.clientUx: Strong for this product class. ContentTab shows requests sorted by needs-action-first with phase progress (requested → brief → review → writing → post review → delivered), 'new' badges for unseen post reviews, and brief HTML export. PostReviewCard is genuinely good: per-field inline TipTap editing with debounced autosave + flush-before-action, saving/saved indicators, approve and request-changes with an explicit steering-feedback textarea and helpful placeholder copy. Edits are correctly restricted to post_review status server-side and snapshot-coalesced for the admin diff. Gaps: clients see raw stacked sections, not a rendered page preview, so it's hard to judge the article as it will appear; no word-count/target or keyword context shown to the client (they review blind on SEO intent); no grounding/evidence shown (a client approving 'factual accuracy' implicitly has nothing to check against); changes_requested gives no visibility into what the team did in response except a status flip; the upgrade-to-full-post path exists but pricing context lives in a separate flow.
maps.aiUsage: Brief path (all GPT, researchMode: true, json): 'content-brief' generate (gpt-5.4, 7000 maxTokens) with ~15 grounding blocks — GSC related queries, GA4 page performance, real provider keyword metrics ('use these exact numbers, do NOT hallucinate'), scraped SERP PAA + top results, scraped reference pages, style examples from top GA4 pages, page-analysis issues, EEAT asset recommendations, insights (cannibalization/decay/quick wins), learnings with availability status, template constraints, page-type + generation-style contracts; 'content-brief-regenerate' and 'content-brief-outline' similar but with only the seoContext slice (3200 token budget). Post path: intro/section/conclusion via callCreativeAI — Claude sonnet-4-6 temp 0.7 with 3 retries, GPT-5.4 fallback — each gets buildSystemPrompt (voice DNA + guardrails + custom notes) + voiceCtx from seoContext+learnings slices + the brief's persisted SERP evidence with explicit 'do not invent source details' framing; 'content-post-unify' (json, temp 0.4, 8–16k tokens) does cohesion + keyword coverage/density caps + structural variety; 'content-post-seo-meta' and 'voice-scoring' on GPT. Review path: 'content-review' on gpt-5.4-mini sees only the first 8000 chars; factual_accuracy/no_hallucinations are hard-coded pass=false with regex-extracted numeric claims handed to the deterministic evidence ledger — a genuinely honest design. Fix path: 'content-post-feedback-fix(-structured)' with Zod-validated shapes and sanitize-then-revalidate. Copy pipeline: 'copy-generation' on Claude with an 8-layer context. Grounding quality is high on the standalone-brief path and notably weaker on the request-driven path (no SERP scrape, no references, no style examples), which is exactly the path clients pay through.
issues: Grounding asymmetry: request-driven briefs (generateBriefForRequest, server/content-brief-generation-job.ts:203-297) never call scrapeSerpData/scrapeUrls — no realPeopleAlsoAsk/realTopResults/referenceUrls are persisted, so buildReviewEvidence (server/routes/content-posts.ts:130-142) returns undefined and every client-requested post's evidence ledger degrades to 'No saved source pack was available'. Client-paid content gets weaker grounding and an empty claim-verification ledger compared to ad-hoc admin briefs.

Auto-publish on approval (server/routes/content-posts.ts:369-441) is a fire-and-forget .then() chain outside the background-job platform: publish failure is only log.warn, the admin UI shows 'Approved' with no error surface, and it skips queueKeywordStrategyPostUpdateFollowOns (only the manual route at server/routes/content-publish.ts:224 enqueues strategy regen) and skips summary/featuredImage field mapping. Two divergent copies of the publish logic exist.

ai-fix for factual_accuracy/no_hallucinations always rewrites post.sections[0] (server/routes/content-posts.ts:792-810) regardless of where the flagged claim lives; the extracted claimsToVerify are never passed into the fix prompt, so the fix and the evidence ledger are disconnected.

internal_links ai-fix fallback prompt says 'Use a plausible internal link like /blog or /services' when the brief has no suggestions (server/routes/content-posts.ts:722) — explicitly invites fabricated URLs, contradicting the platform's hallucination guardrails.

AI review and voice scoring truncate post content to the first 8000 chars (server/routes/content-posts.ts:523, server/content-posts-ai.ts:800) — for 1800+ word posts the tail is never reviewed; brand-voice and internal-links verdicts are computed on a partial document with no disclosure to the admin.

server/copy-voice-feedback.ts (steering-note → voice guardrail suggestion loop) is dead code: zero importers anywhere in server/ or src/. The voice-learning feedback loop it implements never runs, while client steering notes accumulate unanalyzed.

Recommendation generation cannot see the content pipeline: server/recommendations.ts:1091 overrides slices to ['seoContext','clientSignals','learnings'], dropping the default contentPipeline slice — recommendations can duplicate work already in-flight as briefs/posts.

AI review results are never persisted (server/routes/content-posts.ts:578-581 returns them to the UI only) — closing the editor discards the review and claim ledger; re-running re-spends tokens and there is no audit trail that review happened before approval.

Public brief endpoint returns the full internal brief despite the 'client-safe view' comment (server/routes/public-content.ts:502-507) — keywordValidation, templateId, serpAnalysis, difficultyScore and keywordSource internals are exposed to clients, unlike the carefully whitelisted seo-strategy route in the same file.

Brief regeneration creates a new brief id and re-points the request (ContentBriefs.handleRegenerateBrief → request.briefId), but any already-generated post keeps its stale post.briefId — ai-review evidence (buildReviewEvidence reads post.briefId) and the brief→post lineage silently diverge after a regenerate.

generateBrief/regenerateBrief cast every parsed field with bare 'as' casts after parseContentBriefSchema (server/content-brief.ts:890-915, 1475-1500) — the codebase's own #1 bug pattern (guessed shapes compiling through casts) lives at the heart of the brief writer.

Several admin mutation failures are swallowed with console.error and no user feedback (src/components/ContentBriefs.tsx:214,227,235,286,397; src/components/PostEditor.tsx:231) — failed brief edits/status updates look like success.

Brief generation system prompt duplication risk: generateIntroduction/generateSection embed the writer role in both the user prompt and buildSystemPrompt then strip via prompt.replace(role+'\n\n','') (server/content-posts-ai.ts:385,485) — fragile string surgery that silently double-includes the role if the prompt prefix ever changes.
exportPostHTML escapes only '<' in the title and '"' in the meta description (server/content-posts.ts:542-543) — incomplete HTML escaping in the export artifact.
voiceScore/voiceFeedback exist as columns and a route but are never auto-run, never gate anything, never shown to clients, and not aggregated into any slice — vestigial quality signal.
unusedDataOpportunities: Scraped reference/SERP page text (ScrapedPage content) is used once at brief-generation time then discarded — storing it would give the evidence ledger real source text to match claims against instead of title/URL token overlap.

AI review output (per-item verdicts, claimsToVerify, claim-evidence ledger) is never persisted — saving it would create a review audit trail, allow showing clients 'what was verified', and enable measuring which checklist items fail most often per workspace.
voiceScore/voiceFeedback per post are stored but never aggregated: a per-workspace voice-match trend would tell the founder whether calibration is working and could feed the learnings slice.

content_post_versions trigger metadata (client_edit vs manual_edit vs regenerate_section) is a free signal of where AI drafts fail — % of sections clients rewrite, which section types get regenerated — none of it is analyzed or fed into generation style/learnings.

Client steering feedback (clientFeedback on requests, post comments, copy steering notes) is shown to admins but never classified — the already-written copy-voice-feedback.ts module could turn recurring tone complaints into voice guardrail suggestions.

generationStyle (standard/concise/hybrid) is threaded through generation but its outcomes (approval rate, client edits, post-publish performance) are never compared — no data-driven default per workspace.

Published posts have publishedAt + GSC baselines via outcome tracking, but post-level performance is not joined back to the brief attributes that produced it (page type, word count target, SERP-feature directives) — the highest-leverage 'what content works' dataset on the platform is unexploited.
titleVariants/metaDescVariants are generated and stored on every brief but there is no UI to pick a variant on the post and no CTR follow-up — pure token spend today.
contentChecklist (8-10 per-brief verifiable items) is generated but never surfaced as an interactive checklist in PostEditor — only the generic 6-item review checklist exists.

maps.summary: The Client Dashboard is the client-facing portal at /client/:workspaceId/:tab (plus a /client/beta variant that forces effectiveTier=premium and hides all billing UI). A tier- and config-driven nav (src/components/client/client-dashboard/clientDashboardNav.ts) exposes up to 9 tabs — Insights (overview), Performance, Site Health, SEO Strategy, Content Plan, Inbox, Plans, ROI, Brand — backed by ~50 public REST endpoints under /api/public/* with three-layer auth (client JWT, legacy HMAC session cookie, or passwordless-by-URL). It is both a read surface (GSC/GA4 analytics, audits, strategy, recommendations, briefings, outcome wins) and the platform's primary feedback-loop generator: keyword feedback, content-gap votes, business priorities, approval/deliverable responses, content review decisions, and chat-detected intent signals all flow back into the intelligence engine and AI generation paths.

maps.intelligenceWiring: Best-wired surface in the platform. ClientSignalsSlice (server/intelligence/client-signals-slice.ts) is dedicated to this surface and aggregates 15 signal families including a compositeHealthScore (40% churn / 30% ROI / 30% engagement, weight-normalized). It is consumed by: (1) keyword strategy generation — keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:324-344 injects approved/rejected keywords + top rejection reasons; (2) generation-context-builders.ts — clientSignals is a base slice for BOTH buildContentGenerationContext and buildRecommendationGenerationContext; (3) admin chat context. The reverse direction exists too: GET /api/public/intelligence/:workspaceId (client-intelligence.ts) returns a tier-scrubbed ClientIntelligence (insights summary, pipeline status, learnings highlights, rank tracking, SERP opportunities, composite health, weCalledIt, copy pipeline; siteHealth + decay alerts premium-only). Every client feedback write calls invalidateIntelligenceCache + broadcasts INTELLIGENCE_SIGNALS_UPDATED (keyword-feedback.ts:154-162). Gaps: client UI engagement with specific briefing stories/digest items is NOT captured anywhere (no read/click tracking into a slice), and several ClientIntelligence fields (serpOpportunities, copyPipelineStatus) have thin or no client-side rendering.
connectionsOut: SEO Strategy — REAL, strong: keyword feedback POST/bulk/DELETE (public-portal.ts:338-428) → keyword_feedback table → ClientSignalsSlice.keywordFeedback → keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts respects approved/rejected/rejection-reasons on regeneration; notifyKeywordFeedbackChanged invalidates intelligence cache + broadcasts STRATEGY_UPDATED + INTELLIGENCE_SIGNALS_UPDATED. Business priorities POST → client_business_priorities → authority-resolved effectiveBusinessPriorities → strategy generation context. Content-gap votes → content_gap_votes → slice.

Keyword Hub / rank tracking — REAL: client tracked-keyword add/remove (public-content.ts:631/665) writes rank-tracking config; approving a keyword via feedback auto-creates a tracked keyword and broadcasts RANK_TRACKING_UPDATED (public-portal.ts:354-360).

Recommendations — REAL: PATCH /api/public/recommendations/:wsId/:recId (recommendations.ts:145) lets the client move recs through pending→in_progress→completed/dismissed, mirrors affected pages to live page-state, and broadcasts RECOMMENDATIONS_UPDATED; POST /generate triggers the background regeneration job from the client portal.

Action Results (outcome tracking) — REAL: rec completion calls recordAction({sourceType:'recommendation', attribution:'platform_executed', predictedEmv}) + captureBaselineFromGsc (recommendations.ts:199-224) — the client closing the loop IS the outcome-tracking trigger. Approval response patterns also feed ClientSignalsSlice.approvalPatterns.

Content Generation — REAL: brief/post approve / request-changes / inline client-edit / comments (public-content.ts:344-772), per-item flag+edit on deliverables (useUnifiedInbox flaggedItems/editedItems), copy-section approve/suggest (public-portal.ts:718-786), content-plan cell flagging, and paid content-request purchases via Stripe checkout (usePayments + PricingConfirmationModal) — all state-machine-guarded and broadcast.

Admin awareness — REAL: chat intent classification creates client_signals rows + CLIENT_SIGNAL_CREATED broadcast + team email (public-analytics.ts:540-560); every portal mutation writes client_* activity types that feed engagement metrics and churn signals.
connectionsIn: SEO Strategy — REAL: GET /api/public/seo-strategy (public-content.ts:127-253) is an explicit whitelist projection of the assembled strategy (pageMap with positions/volume/cpc, contentGaps with opportunityScore + backfilled honesty flag, quickWins with roiScore, keywordGaps capped at 20, topicClusters, cannibalization, strategyUx). Raw CPC on gaps and provider internals deliberately withheld.

Keyword Hub — REAL but thin: rank-tracking /history and /latest endpoints feed PerformanceTab; rankTrackingSummary in ClientIntelligence. The new admin Keyword Hub (flag 'keyword-hub') has NO dedicated client surface — clients see only the strategy projection and tracked-keyword list, none of the Evaluated/command-center depth.

Recommendations — REAL: GET public recommendations with emvPerWeek/predictedEmv/roiPerEffortDay stripped and estimatedGain $-sanitized (recommendations.ts:50-71); topRecommendationId drives the Overview '#1 priority' card with component-contribution bars; full set renders in InsightsEngine inside the Health tab.

Action Results — PARTIAL: /api/public/outcomes/:id/wins feeds WinsSurface (briefing-v2 only) and weCalledIt feeds PredictionShowcaseCard (legacy overview); learningHighlights in ClientIntelligence. BUT /api/public/outcomes/:id/summary (the scorecard) has NO live consumer — OutcomeSummary.tsx is dead code, so the quantified 'what did our work achieve' scorecard never reaches clients on either layout.

Content Generation — REAL: briefs/posts/copy arrive in Inbox Reviews (ContentTab via ProjectedReviewModal, PostReviewCard, ClientCopyReview); content-performance endpoints exist for published-content trends; content plan matrices render read-only with review cells routed to inbox.

Insights/analytics engine — REAL: /insights/narrative (client-framed via buildClientInsights), /insights/digest (AI monthly digest), raw /insights, plus the weekly briefing assembled server-side from insights + content gaps + weCalledIt stories (briefing-cron.ts).
maps.adminUx: This surface has no single admin control panel — admin-side levers are scattered: per-workspace visibility toggles (seoClientView, analyticsClientView, siteIntelligenceClientView, clientPortalEnabled, billingMode) live in workspace settings; what appears in the client inbox is driven by 'Send to client' actions on each of the other five surfaces; briefings have a cron + auto-publish setting (autoPublishBriefings/autoPublishAfterHours) rather than a clear weekly review queue surface. The admin cannot preview the client portal as a specific tier without changing workspace state (beta mode forces premium, but there is no 'view as free/growth' switch). Client engagement visibility for the admin is decent (client_signals chips, activity log entries, churn signals from the slice) but fragmented across AdminChat context and activity feeds rather than a single 'client engagement' view. The flag-gated briefing-v2 means the admin must mentally track which clients see the magazine layout vs the legacy overview — two different products under one URL.
maps.clientUx: Strong skeleton, uneven depth. Strengths: the action-needed banner aggregates approvals/briefs/posts/replies/plan-reviews with correct inbox deep-links (?tab= contract wired end-to-end); the '#1 priority' card with contribution bars genuinely answers 'what should I do next'; unified inbox cleanly separates Decisions/Reviews/Conversations with inline approve for single actions and a bulk modal for batches; empty states are mostly action-oriented; SeoEducationTip + Explainer glossary lower the jargon barrier; loading states are contextual. Weaknesses: (1) the legacy overview is overloaded — health score + 5 stat cards + up to 3 stacked banners + monthly digest + intelligence card + predictions + insight feed + 3 sidebar cards compete for attention; (2) the chat advisor disappears entirely for workspaces with no GSC/GA4/audit data (ClientChatWidget.tsx:90) — exactly the onboarding window when a client has questions; (3) nav items appear/vanish based on data (ROI requires strategyData, Content Plan requires cells) with no explanation of why a tab a client saw last week is gone; (4) free tier gets NO Inbox nav item, yet the overview banner can deep-link into /inbox — pending approvals for free workspaces are discoverable only via that banner; (5) a paid client whose admin disabled seoClientView sees a locked Strategy tab whose click opens the tier UpgradeModal (clientDashboardNav.ts strategyLocked + ClientHeader locked→setShowUpgradeModal) — upgrading would not unlock it, a genuinely misleading affordance; (6) section errors collapse to generic 'try refreshing the page' lines; (7) HealthScoreCard shows a composite number whose churn/ROI/engagement composition is never explained to the client.
maps.aiUsage: Four AI paths serve this surface. (1) Client SEO Advisor chat — POST /api/public/search-chat (public-analytics.ts:305-580): gpt-5.4 with operation 'client-search-chat', a large persona prompt with revenue hooks (beta-safe variant), educator mode, strict no-content-writing/no-competitor/no-pricing rules, plus a parallel gpt-5.4-nano intent classifier whose result creates client_signals and emails the team. Grounding is mixed: server-side blocks (audit-traffic intelligence, content plan, buildSeoPromptContext from generation-context-builders) are well-grounded, but the bulk of the data context is the FRONTEND-supplied req.body.context (z.record(z.unknown()), no size cap) serialized verbatim into the system prompt — the client controls what the AI believes is 'their data', a prompt-injection and token-cost surface.
