(2) Monthly digest — generateMonthlyDigest (monthly-digest.ts): gpt-5.4 summary grounded in insights slice, outcome ROI highlights, search/GA4 comparisons, via buildSystemPrompt; 24h cache + in-flight dedup. (3) Weekly briefing — deterministic templates from insights/content-gaps/weCalledIt (briefing-templates/), with AI only as optional premium polish (hero-headline punch + weekly opener in briefing-prompt.ts, gpt via callAI, multiline/quote-stripping guards) — a good deterministic-first design. (4) Chat session auto-summarization after 6+ messages. Tier gating on chat is enforced server-side (checkChatRateLimit) and free-tier caps surface in the widget UI.
issues: Dead code, ~1,200+ lines: src/components/client/OutcomeSummary.tsx (277L), WeCalledIt.tsx (228L), ClientActionDetailModal.tsx, FixRecommendations.tsx, and InsightCards.tsx are imported nowhere; useClientOutcomeSummary and GET /api/public/outcomes/:workspaceId/summary (server/routes/outcomes.ts:368) have zero UI consumers — the client-facing outcome scorecard was built and never mounted.

Auth gap in public-portal.ts: unlike public-content.ts:64 and public-analytics.ts:71 (router-level requireClientPortalAuth), public-portal.ts applies middleware per-route and leaves GET /api/public/audit-summary/:workspaceId (line 246), GET /keyword-feedback/:workspaceId (331), GET /business-priorities/:workspaceId (459), and GET /content-gap-votes/:workspaceId (622) fully unauthenticated — for a password-protected workspace, anyone with the UUID can read audit scores, the client's keyword approvals/rejections with reasons, and stated business priorities.

Prompt-injection / unbounded-cost vector: /api/public/search-chat injects client-supplied req.body.context (validated only as z.record(z.unknown()) — no size limit, no schema) directly into the system prompt via JSON.stringify (public-analytics.ts:300-303, 478). A client can fabricate data the AI will assert as fact, and chatDeps re-serializes the entire dashboard dataset (all GA4/GSC/audit/strategy payloads) on every message.

Misleading lock affordance: paid workspace with seoClientView=false renders Strategy as locked (clientDashboardNav.ts:31 strategyLocked) and ClientHeader routes locked clicks to the tier UpgradeModal — but the lock is an admin visibility toggle, not a tier gate; upgrading cannot unlock it.

Free-tier inbox incoherence: buildClientDashboardNav omits Inbox for free tier entirely, yet ClientDashboardTabContent renders the InboxTab panel for any deep-linked tab with no tier guard, and OverviewTab's action banner links free users into it — approvals sent to free workspaces are reachable only through that one banner path.
ContentPlanTab download mismatch (ContentPlanTab.tsx:62-64): the UI offers 'docx'/'pdf' but the handler maps docx→csv and pdf→json query params — users get the wrong file format with no indication.

ClientDashboard.tsx is an 842-line god component that fires ~20 React Query hooks at bootstrap regardless of active tab (copy entries, content plan, pricing, page keywords, annotations, anomalies all load on the overview), and getInsights() re-filters query arrays on every render unmemoized.

Two parallel overview implementations (legacy OverviewTab+InsightsDigest ≈1,270 lines vs Briefing/ suite ≈1,500 lines) behind 'client-briefing-v2' which defaults to false (shared/types/feature-flags.ts:27) — double maintenance surface and divergent client experiences; the weekly briefing cron only runs for flag-enabled paid workspaces, so the briefing data pipeline is dark for most clients.

ClientChatWidget.tsx:90 returns null when overview, audit, and ga4Overview are all absent — new clients in the setup window (precisely when the empty-state tells them what's coming) have no way to ask questions.

KNOWN_CLIENT_TABS (client-dashboard-tab.ts) still includes 'approvals'/'requests'/'content' which have no panels — only the App.tsx:128 splat redirect prevents a blank page; the coupling is undocumented in either file.
unusedDataOpportunities: Outcome scorecard: /api/public/outcomes/:id/summary already computes a client-safe win/loss scorecard but nothing renders it — a 'results this quarter' module on the overview or ROI tab is essentially free.
compositeHealthScore components: the slice computes churn/ROI/engagement sub-scores but HealthScoreCard shows only the bare number — the breakdown would make the score actionable instead of opaque.
ClientIntelligence.serpOpportunities and copyPipelineStatus are assembled and returned per-request but have little/no dedicated client rendering — wasted slice assembly cost.

Client engagement telemetry (portalUsage.featuresUsed, loginFrequency) flows only to admin churn signals — it could drive in-portal nudges (e.g., 'you have 3 unreviewed approvals older than 7 days' exists in briefing-v2 staleness but not in the legacy layout most clients see).

Briefing/digest story engagement is never tracked: no record of which stories a client opened or drilled into, so the briefing generator cannot learn which story types clients value — an obvious ClientSignalsSlice extension.
Chat session summaries (auto-generated after 6 messages) are stored but only the admin slice reads recent topics — the client widget could resurface 'last time you asked about X' continuity.

Content-gap votes are collected and shown to the AI, but the briefing's RecommendedForYou and strategy contentGaps ranking do not visibly incorporate the client's own upvotes — the client's stated preferences don't observably reorder what they're recommended.

anomalies are fetched on every client bootstrap (useClientAnomalies) but AnomalyAlerts was removed from the overview (ClientDashboard.tsx:26 comment) — the query result now feeds only chatDeps, an expensive way to populate a chat context field.

analyses.summary: The six surfaces are wired far better than a typical codebase — strategy grounds recommendations/content/client views via the seoContext slice, client feedback genuinely reaches strategy prompts, and rec completions flow into outcome tracking with GSC baselines. But the back half of the intended loop (actions → measured results → learnings → better recommendations/content) is the weakest part: the learnings edge exists and is consumed everywhere, yet the data flowing across it is systematically corrupted (fabricated scores for ~6 action types, unexecuted suggestions scored as wins/losses, backfilled rec completions all mislabeled audit_fix_applied, difficulty multipliers calibrated on position bins). Three whole edges are missing or phantom: Keyword Hub lifecycle decisions never create tracked actions, strategy regenerations are outcome-invisible after the first run ever, and the Hub's ADD_TO_STRATEGY action shows "in strategy" without writing the strategy. The recommendations engine deliberately drops the contentPipeline slice and reads no briefs/posts, so it cannot see in-flight content, content recs have no path into brief creation, and the client-paid brief path skips both SERP grounding and outcome recording. Net effect: the platform compounds intelligence on the forward path (data→strategy→content) but the feedback path that would make recommendations measurably smarter over time is either starved or poisoned.

findings.title: Learnings edge transmits fabricated outcome scores into every downstream AI/scoring consumer
findings.detail: The actions→learnings→recommendations/content edge is fully wired (LearningsSlice consumed by buildContentGenerationContext, buildRecommendationGenerationContext, strategy synthesis, AdminChat) but the data crossing it is corrupted at three verified points. (1) server/outcome-scoring-defaults.ts:27-46 assigns primary metrics that no GSC snapshot ever contains — content_refreshed→'click_recovery', internal_link_added→'target_improvement', brief_created→'content_produced' — and computeDelta (server/outcome-measurement.ts:183-198) reads missing fields as 0 vs 0, so these action types can only ever score neutral; audit_fix_applied→'page_health_score' and voice_calibrated→'voice_score' exist in baselines but not in current GSC fetches, scoring systematic −100% losses. (2) getPendingMeasurement (server/outcome-tracking.ts:58) has no attribution filter, so 'not_acted_on' suggestion rows (content-decay and internal-link suggestions recorded as unexecuted) are scored as if executed and pollute win rates. (3) workspace-learnings.ts:207-226 bins 'winRateByDifficultyRange' from baseline GSC POSITION, but buildOutcomeAdjustment (server/outcome-learning-default-path.ts:65-79) matches real provider keyword DIFFICULTY against those bins — the multiplier compares incommensurate quantities. These corrupted learnings then multiply recommendation rank scores (server/recommendations.ts via outcomeRankScores), keyword candidate scores (server/keyword-recommendations.ts), and are asserted as fact in strategy/brief prompts.
findings.recommendation: Fix the edge at the source: (a) make primary metrics for content_refreshed/internal_link_added/brief_created resolve to real GSC fields (clicks/position) or mark those types measurement-exempt; (b) add `AND attribution != 'not_acted_on'` to getPendingMeasurement and the learnings aggregation filter; (c) either bin winRateByDifficultyRange from stored provider difficulty on the action context or rename/rekey it as position-based and stop matching provider difficulty against it. Until fixed, consider hard-disabling the difficulty multiplier in buildOutcomeAdjustment.

findings.title: Backfill cron mislabels every completed recommendation as audit_fix_applied, poisoning the rec-type calibration loop
findings.detail: Verified: server/outcome-backfill.ts (~line 197) hardcodes actionType: 'audit_fix_applied' for every completed recommendation it backfills, with estimated captured_at-only baselines and predictedEmv: null. Meanwhile the platform-executed completion paths (resolveRecommendationsForChange callers in server/work-orders.ts:200, server/domains/inbox/approval-batch-apply.ts:209, server/routes/webflow*.ts, plus auto-resolve in recommendations.ts) never call recordAction with sourceType 'recommendation' themselves — so the weekly backfill IS the action-creation path for most completions. The compile-time-exhaustive recommendationOutcomeActionType mapping (server/recommendations.ts:198-242) exists precisely to keep winRateByActionType honest, and this cron bypasses it wholesale: content_gap/keyword_gap/topic_cluster/local rec outcomes all land in the audit_fix_applied bucket, which then feeds buildOutcomeAdjustment rank multipliers for FUTURE recommendations of every type. The closed loop the architecture advertises (rec → outcome → calibrated next rec) is closed but mis-addressed.
findings.recommendation: In outcome-backfill.ts, derive actionType from the rec via the existing recommendationOutcomeActionType(rec.type, rec.source) mapping instead of the hardcoded literal. Separately, have resolveRecommendationsForChange/auto-resolve record properly-typed actions at completion time (with real GSC baselines) so the estimated-baseline backfill becomes the recovery net it claims to be.

findings.title: Recommendations engine is blind to the content pipeline it triggers work in
findings.detail: Verified two halves. (1) server/recommendations.ts:1085-1092 calls buildRecommendationGenerationContext with an explicit slices override ['seoContext','clientSignals','learnings'], dropping the contentPipeline slice that is in the builder's defaults; grep confirms recommendations.ts contains zero reads of content_briefs, content_posts, or the contentPipeline slice. The content_gap branch (recommendations.ts:1381-1393) filters only client-declined keywords. So the engine will mint a content_gap rec for a keyword that already has a brief in progress or a post awaiting review. (2) Publishing a post never resolves a matching content_gap rec: the full caller list of resolveRecommendationsForChange/ForPageIds (work-orders, approval-batch-apply, webflow SEO routes/jobs) excludes both publish paths (server/routes/content-publish.ts, server/routes/content-posts.ts); the gap rec only disappears when a future STRATEGY regeneration removes the row from content_gaps. The forward edge (strategy gaps → recs) is strong; the reconciliation edge (content produced → rec closed/deduped) does not exist.
findings.recommendation: Remove the slices override (or add 'contentPipeline') and skip/annotate content_gap recs whose targetKeyword matches an existing brief/post targetKeyword (content-pipeline-slice already computes coverage gaps — reuse it). On publish (both routes), resolve content_gap recs whose targetKeyword matches the published post's targetKeyword.

findings.title: Content recommendations dead-end: no edge from a content rec into brief/request creation
findings.detail: Verified: mapToProduct (server/recommendations.ts:879-902) has cases for metadata/schema/accessibility/aeo/content_refresh and `default: return {}` — recs of type 'content' (content_gap, keyword_gap, topic_cluster sources, see :1418) get no productType, so the client InsightsEngine renders only 'I'll Handle This' with no purchase or brief-request CTA, and no admin affordance creates a brief or content_request from a rec. This breaks the loop at its highest-value link: strategy → recommendation → content. The strategy surface has this edge (client gap CTAs create paid content requests via StrategyContentOpportunitiesSection; admin ContentGaps rows carry Draft Brief buttons), so the recommendation surface re-surfaces the same gaps WITHOUT the conversion path that already exists one tab over.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'content' case to mapToProduct (reusing the strategy tab's brief/post pricing from contentPricing) or wire the rec card CTA to the same content-request creation flow StrategyContentOpportunitiesSection uses, carrying targetKeyword/topic/suggestedPageType from the rec into the request.

findings.title: Auto-publish-on-approval skips the strategy/recommendation refresh that manual publish triggers
findings.detail: Verified: the manual publish route calls queueKeywordStrategyPostUpdateFollowOns({workspaceId}) at server/routes/content-publish.ts:224 (which queues llms.txt regen + delayed recommendation regen, server/keyword-strategy-follow-ons.ts:68-78). The auto-publish path embedded in the PATCH status handler (server/routes/content-posts.ts:369-441, a fire-and-forget .then() chain) records the outcome action and broadcasts CONTENT_PUBLISHED but never imports or calls queueKeywordStrategyPostUpdateFollowOns — grep confirms zero references in content-posts.ts. So for any workspace with publishTarget configured (the intended steady-state), every approval-published post leaves recommendations and llms.txt stale until some unrelated trigger fires. Two divergent copies of publish logic also drift on field mapping (auto path skips summary/featuredImage).
findings.recommendation: Extract a single publishPostToWebflow function used by both routes (ideally as a background job per docs/rules/background-generation.md) that always ends with the outcome action + follow-ons queue, eliminating the divergence.

findings.title: Strategy generation is outcome-invisible after the first run ever — the strategy half of the measurement loop is structurally empty
findings.detail: Verified at server/keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:184-193: `if (!getActionBySource('strategy', ws.id)) recordAction({...})` — exactly one strategy_keyword_added tracked action per workspace, forever, with sourceId = workspace id, pageUrl: null, targetKeyword: null, and a captured_at-only baseline. Every subsequent regeneration (the events whose impact you would actually measure) produces no tracked action, and the one action that exists has no page URL so GSC measurement can never score it (and workspace-learnings strategy aggregation requires ≥10 strategy items to compute anything, workspace-learnings.ts:205). Result: 'learningsDomain: strategy' injected into strategy synthesis prompts (keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:297) is computed over an essentially empty or noise-only population — the strategy→results→learnings→strategy loop is drawn on the diagram but carries ~no data.
findings.recommendation: Record one action per regeneration (sourceId = generation timestamp or strategy_history row id), and more usefully record per-keyword-addition actions for net-new pageMap primary keywords (pageUrl from pageMap) so position-based scoring actually works. Drop the once-ever guard.

findings.title: Keyword Hub ↔ Action Results edge is entirely missing in both directions
findings.detail: Verified: grep for recordAction in server/keyword-command-center.ts returns zero hits — no Hub lifecycle action (TRACK, PROMOTE_EVIDENCE, ADD_TO_STRATEGY, RETIRE, DECLINE) creates a tracked action. Tracked-keyword baselines (baselinePosition/baselineClicks/baselineImpressions) are written at track-time (server/tracked-keywords-store.ts:212-240, server/rank-tracking.ts:306) but their only consumers are rank-tracking/KCC display modules (keyword-command-center.ts:1235,2396, keyword-strategy-ux.ts:276) — no outcome module reads them. outcome-measurement.ts measures exclusively via page-level GSC (getPageTrend), never via the per-keyword rank_snapshots the scheduler captures daily. So the question the Hub exists to answer over time — 'did promoting/tracking this keyword pay off?' — is structurally unanswerable, and keyword-level decisions contribute nothing to workspace learnings. The reverse direction (outcome annotations on keyword rows, e.g. 'brief for this keyword shipped and won') is also absent.
findings.recommendation: Record a tracked action on TRACK/PROMOTE_EVIDENCE/ADD_TO_STRATEGY with targetKeyword + baselinePosition, and add a keyword-level measurement path that scores against rank_snapshots (the data already exists and is captured daily). This is the cheapest unbuilt edge with the highest learning payoff.

findings.title: ADD_TO_STRATEGY is a phantom edge: Hub shows 'in strategy' without writing the strategy
findings.detail: Verified at server/keyword-command-center.ts:3287-3295: the ADD_TO_STRATEGY case writes upsertFeedback(...,'approved') and upserts a tracked keyword with source STRATEGY_SITE_KEYWORD — it never touches page_keywords, keywordStrategy.siteKeywords, or any strategy table. Actual inclusion depends on a FUTURE AI regeneration honoring the 'APPROVED KEYWORDS' prompt section (keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:344-346), which is a soft hint, not a guarantee. Meanwhile the Hub's lifecycle resolution immediately labels the row IN_STRATEGY and the success message says 'added to the strategy operating loop'. The Strategy surface, the client strategy projection, and recommendations (which read strategy tables) all disagree with the Hub until the next regeneration happens — and the MCP tool add_keyword_to_strategy proves a direct page_keywords write path already exists (server/mcp/tools/keyword-actions.ts).
findings.recommendation: Make ADD_TO_STRATEGY write the artifact deterministically (reuse the MCP add_keyword_to_strategy page_keywords write, or append to siteKeywords + site_keyword_metrics) inside the same transaction, keeping the feedback row as the AI-regeneration signal. Alternatively, label the row honestly ('approved — joins strategy on next generation').

findings.title: Client-paid (request-driven) brief path skips SERP/reference grounding AND outcome recording — the weakest generation path is the one clients pay for
findings.detail: Verified by function boundaries in server/content-brief-generation-job.ts: generateStandaloneBrief (:73-201) scrapes references, SERP data, and style examples (:114-142) and records the brief_created outcome action (:176). generateBriefForRequest (:203-309) — the path used for every client content request — does neither: it enriches with GSC/provider/GA4/decay context but contains no scrapeSerpData/scrapeUrls calls and no recordAction. Downstream consequences: (a) request-driven briefs persist no realPeopleAlsoAsk/realTopResults/referenceUrls, so buildReviewEvidence (server/routes/content-posts.ts:130-142) returns undefined and the claim-verification evidence ledger for every client-requested post degrades to 'no saved source pack'; (b) client-requested briefs are invisible to outcome tracking, so the learnings system cannot compare paid vs ad-hoc content performance. The data→content grounding edge and the content→results edge are both severed precisely on the revenue path.
findings.recommendation: Lift the scrape block (and the recordAction call) out of generateStandaloneBrief into shared code both paths call; the request path already has targetKeyword and runs inside the same background job, so cost/latency parity is acceptable.

findings.title: Keyword Hub's read model (lifecycle states, value scores, rank history) is invisible to the intelligence layer
findings.detail: Verified: grep for 'keyword-command-center' across server/intelligence/ returns zero hits, and the only keyword-tracking signal in any slice is SeoContextSlice.rankTracking = {trackedKeywords count, avgPosition, positionChanges} (server/intelligence/seo-context-slice.ts:113-141), rendered as roughly one prompt line. The Hub's 3,563-line read model computes lifecycle status (needs_review/retired/declined), Layer-1 value scores, dollar values, variant rollups, and per-keyword rank history — none of which reaches any slice, so AdminChat, brief generation, and recommendation context builders cannot answer 'which keywords need review' or 'what's our top opportunity by value' despite the server computing exactly that for the UI on every page load. This is a partial Data Flow Rule 6 violation (CLAUDE.md: 'The AI context and AdminChat are blind to data that isn't wired into a slice') and a regenerate-from-scratch smell: the richest keyword dataset on the platform is UI-only.
findings.recommendation: Add a compact keywordLifecycle section to SeoContextSlice (or a thin dedicated slice) carrying segment counts, top-5 opportunities by value score with dollar estimates, and needs_review count — assembled from the same source bundle the Hub already builds, behind the existing intelligence cache.

findings.title: Recommendation state never flows back upstream: dismissals teach nothing and strategy generation reads zero rec state
findings.detail: Two verified one-way edges that should be loops. (1) Strategy synthesis (server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts) contains no read of recommendation_sets or rec status — its only 'recommendation' reference is a prompt string at :1301 — so a client repeatedly dismissing content_gap recs for a topic has no effect on the next strategy generation re-proposing the same gaps (only keyword-level declines are respected, via keyword_feedback). (2) Dismissal carries no reason and feeds no learning store: the merge preserves dismissed status (recommendations.ts:2276-2280) but nothing aggregates dismissal patterns by rec type/source into clientSignals or learnings, even though the exact pattern exists for keywords (declined keywords are hard-filtered at recommendations.ts:1385 and injected as hard constraints into strategy prompts). Pending recs also get fresh random ids each regen (only in_progress/completed/dismissed preserve ids, recommendations.ts:2265-2281), so briefing candidates and client-side references to pending recs dangle across regens, weakening the recommendations↔client-dashboard edge.
findings.recommendation: Mirror the declined-keywords pattern: persist dismissals (type, source key, optional reason) in a small table, filter/down-rank matching candidates at mint time, and surface dismissal patterns in ClientSignalsSlice. Derive pending rec ids from buildMergeKey instead of random ids so references survive regens.

findings.title: Predicted-vs-realized EMV calibration loop is collected but never closed, and the rare path is the only one that pairs data
findings.detail: Verified: tracked_actions.predicted_emv accrues (migration 116) but only the manual client PATCH completion path snapshots it (server/routes/recommendations.ts:216); the backfill path that creates actions for all platform-executed completions explicitly writes predictedEmv: null (server/outcome-backfill.ts:209-211, with a comment acknowledging it). ov-calibration.ts's own header (:32-36) states the realized-vs-predicted pairing 'now accrues' toward a P6 median(attributedValue/predictedEmv) calibration — but since most completions flow through backfill, the dataset accrues almost exclusively from the rarest completion path. Combined with attributed_value being invisible in all admin UI, the 'are our dollar projections honest?' loop — the credibility backbone of the OV scoring that ranks everything clients see — has no foreseeable convergence. Similarly, suggestPlaybook (server/outcome-playbooks.ts) has zero callers anywhere (verified grep), so discovered winning action sequences never influence recommendation generation.
findings.recommendation: Snapshot predictedEmv at action-creation time for every rec-sourced action (the rec object with opportunity.value is in hand at resolveRecommendationsForChange/backfill read time via merge-key lookup), not just manual PATCH. Schedule the P6 calibration switch once N≥threshold pairs exist; wire suggestPlaybook into the rec engine as a candidate source or delete it.

findings.title: Strategy persists intelligence that nothing consumes: searchSignals, competitorKeywordData, generation telemetry, and 4 of 5 history snapshots are write-only
findings.detail: Verified: grep for 'searchSignals' across src/ returns zero consumers, yet it is persisted into the workspace blob on every generation (server/keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:115-121) — device/country mix, period comparison, GA4 organic overview written to every workspace and shipped in every admin GET for nothing. strategy_history retains 5 snapshots per workspace (persistence:176-179) but all read paths use only the latest (keyword-strategy-ux.ts:55 LIMIT 1; the diff route; seo-context-slice.ts:25 reads only generated_at timestamps) — strategy evolution over time is invisible to both the UI and the intelligence layer, so the AI cannot reason about whether past strategy changes worked. generationQuality telemetry is computed then only logged (keyword-strategy-generation.ts:588-602). These are not bugs, but they are paid-for data (provider credits, tokens, storage) feeding no edge in the loop.
findings.recommendation: Either wire these into consumers (a search-snapshot card from searchSignals; a strategy-evolution trend from history into the seoContext slice; persisted generationQuality rows for a generation-health view) or stop persisting them. Prioritize the history trend — 'did the last strategy change improve coverage/positions' is a direct compounding-intelligence input.

analyses.dimension: ux-admin (Questions 2+7, admin half): usefulness and usability for a solo founder operating many client workspaces
analyses.summary: Per-workspace depth is strong (background jobs with progress, good empty/loading states, primitives used consistently), but admin throughput across many workspaces is bottlenecked at exactly the points a solo founder hits daily. Cross-workspace triage half-exists: WorkspaceOverview aggregates "Needs Attention" items but the rows are dead text with no drill-down, and the only other cross-workspace view is the outcomes overview. Several surfaces dead-end one level down — Recommendations has no admin management view at all (the admin borrows a compact client component with tier hardcoded to "premium"), Action Results lets you filter by score but never shows scores and its per-action drill-down/note hooks were built and never mounted. The platform also makes the admin remember things it already knows (client keyword requests shown only as counts, no nudge when feedback accumulates since the last strategy generation, auto-publish failures visible only in server logs) and swallows mutation errors in the content pipeline so failed edits look like successes. The flagged Keyword Hub cutover will regress two workflows (one-click force-bypass of protected keywords; loss of the only manual add-keyword input) unless fixed before the P5 flip.

findings.title: Cross-workspace 'Needs Attention' items are dead text with no drill-down
findings.detail: src/components/WorkspaceOverview.tsx:110-124 renders the Needs Attention list (new requests, pending approvals, briefs awaiting review, work orders, rejected changes, low health, churn risk) as plain divs with a label and a zinc Badge — no onClick, no per-workspace attribution, no deep-link. The admin sees '3 pending approvals' but must visually scan every workspace card below (line 183 hasAlerts border tinting is the only per-workspace cue) and then navigate into each workspace to find the actual item. For a solo founder with many workspaces this is the single landing-page triage surface, and it answers 'something needs attention' without answering 'where' or 'open it'.
findings.recommendation: Make each attention item expandable into the per-workspace breakdown with direct deep-links (adminPath(wsId, tab) already exists for every item type: approvals → PendingApprovals, content → seo-briefs, requests → requests). Sort workspace cards by attention severity instead of relying on border color alone.

findings.title: No admin recommendations management surface — admin borrows the client component with tier hardcoded to 'premium'
findings.detail: The admin's only recommendations view is a compact client InsightsEngine embed: src/components/WorkspaceHome.tsx:628 mounts <InsightsEngine workspaceId tier="premium" compact>. Full mode mounts only inside ClientDashboard.tsx:683. Verified by grep: those are the only two mount points. There is no admin UI to inspect the full queue, view/undo dismissed recs (the state machine allows un-dismiss but nothing reaches it), inspect per-rec OV breakdowns, or suppress/reorder for a client. Additionally server/routes/recommendations.ts contains zero addActivity calls (verified by grep), so client complete/dismiss actions never appear in the activity feed — the admin is blind to clients triaging their own plan.
findings.recommendation: Add a full admin recommendations view (or full-mode InsightsEngine with an admin variant) per workspace including dismissed-recs visibility and OV component breakdowns; add addActivity on the public PATCH/DELETE routes so client triage shows up in the activity feed and WorkspaceOverview recent activity.

findings.title: Action Results dead-ends one level down: scores filterable but never displayed, per-action drill-down and notes built but never mounted
findings.detail: src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomeActionFeed.tsx:175-202 offers a score filter, but rows never render the outcome score or checkpoint — the only 'result' column reads action.trailingHistory (line 54-69), which is never populated by any recordAction caller (server/outcome-tracking.ts:209 always writes the empty default; verified by grep that no caller passes trailingHistory), so the delta is permanently blank. The per-action endpoint and note mutation are fully implemented server-side and hook-side but useOutcomeAction/useAddOutcomeNote in src/hooks/admin/useOutcomes.ts have zero component consumers (verified). The type-filter dropdown (OutcomeActionFeed.tsx:12-24) lists 10 types while ACTION_TYPE_LABELS (src/components/admin/outcomes/outcomeConstants.ts:16-32) defines 15 — competitor_gap_closed, cluster_published, cannibalization_resolved, local_visibility_won, local_service_added cannot be filtered to. Net: the admin cannot answer 'what happened to this specific action and why' from the UI that exists to answer exactly that.
findings.recommendation: Render the latest outcome score + checkpoint on each feed row (data is one join away via the existing /actions/:id endpoint); mount an action detail drawer wired to the already-written useOutcomeAction/useAddOutcomeNote; sync ACTION_TYPE_OPTIONS to ACTION_TYPE_LABELS; delete or populate trailingHistory.

findings.title: Keyword Hub silently force-bypasses protected-keyword confirmation in one click
findings.detail: src/components/keyword-command-center/KeywordActionMenu.tsx:90 passes { force: true } whenever a lifecycle action has disabledReason, and src/components/KeywordHub.tsx:340-351 does the same in the drawer handler — the inline comment explicitly says it force-flags so 'a protected retire/decline/pause would throw instead of applying'. This means a pinned, client-requested, or strategy-owned keyword can be retired/declined in a single click with no confirmation, defeating the server-side protection contract by design. The legacy KeywordCommandCenter shell routed protected actions through a ConfirmDialog; the new Hub kept confirmation only on the bulk path and hard-delete. Flag is OFF in production today (shared/types/feature-flags.ts:37), so this ships at the P5 cutover.
findings.recommendation: Before the keyword-hub flag flips: replace auto-force with a ConfirmDialog that explains why the keyword is protected (disabledReason is already the right copy) and passes force:true only on explicit confirm — mirroring the bulk path's protected-selection dialog.

findings.title: Keyword Hub cutover removes the only manual add-keyword input
findings.detail: With the keyword-hub flag ON, src/App.tsx:450 redirects seo-ranks → seo-keywords, making RankTracker.tsx:402-408 — the only 'Add keyword to track...' input in the admin UI (verified by grep across KeywordHub.tsx, keyword-hub/, keyword-command-center/) — unreachable. The Hub shell has only a search/filter box. A net-new keyword with no GSC/strategy/local evidence (e.g. a keyword the client mentioned on a call) cannot enter tracking from the admin UI at all post-cutover; the admin would have to use the client portal's tracked-keywords flow or MCP.
findings.recommendation: Add an 'Add keyword' affordance to the Hub header (reusing the rank-tracking add mutation that RankTracker already calls) before the P5 cutover deletes RankTracker.

findings.title: Webflow auto-publish on approval fails invisibly — admin sees 'Approved', publish silently dies
findings.detail: server/routes/content-posts.ts:369-441: approval triggers a fire-and-forget createCollectionItem(...).then(...) chain outside the background-job platform. On failure the only trace is log.warn ('Auto-publish failed for ...') — no activity-log entry, no WS broadcast, no job row, no UI surface. The admin (and client) see the post as approved with no indication it never reached the live site. The path also diverges from the manual publish route: it skips queueKeywordStrategyPostUpdateFollowOns (present only in server/routes/content-publish.ts:224, verified by grep), so approval-published posts never trigger the strategy/recommendation refresh that manually published posts do.
findings.recommendation: Move auto-publish into the background job platform (it already has the // background-generation-ok hatch comment admitting this is interim) so failures surface in TaskPanel; at minimum write an addActivity 'auto-publish failed' entry and broadcast it; call queueKeywordStrategyPostUpdateFollowOns on success to match the manual route.

findings.title: Ten swallowed mutation failures in the admin content pipeline — failed edits look like success
findings.detail: src/components/ContentBriefs.tsx has 10 catch blocks that only console.error ('ContentBriefs operation failed:') with no toast or error state (lines 214, 227, 235, 256, 269, 286, 344, 365, 384, 397 — verified by grep), covering saveBriefField, regenerate brief/outline, and request status updates. An admin can edit a brief field, hit a 400, and see nothing — the optimistic cache keeps showing the old/edited value until refetch. The same component is a 28-useState mega-surface hosting the generator form, brief list, request list, generated-posts list, and PostEditor; posts simultaneously have a second home in ContentManager.tsx with different affordances ('Send to client' lives there), so the same post has two inconsistent admin views.
findings.recommendation: Route these mutations through useMutation with onError toasts (the Toast component exists); medium-term, split ContentBriefs and consolidate the two post lists into one surface so 'where do I manage posts' has one answer.

findings.title: AI post review results are never persisted — token spend and verification evidence evaporate on editor close
findings.detail: server/routes/content-posts.ts:570-581: the AI review verdicts plus the claim/evidence ledger are returned only in res.json — nothing is written to content_posts or anywhere else (verified: no DB write between safeParse and res.json). Closing PostEditor discards the review; re-opening requires a fresh paid AI call; there is no audit trail that review happened before a post was approved, and the provenance-sensitive items the system deliberately force-fails for human verification leave no record of that human verification either.
findings.recommendation: Persist the latest review result + evidence ledger on the post row (a JSON column alongside reviewChecklist), stamp reviewedAt, and render it on reopen — this turns a repeated token cost into a one-time audit artifact and lets review status appear in the posts list.

findings.title: Client keyword requests reach the admin as counts only, and 'Add to strategy' doesn't actually add to the strategy
findings.detail: Two halves of one broken loop. (1) The KeywordStrategy admin panel's 'Client Keyword Feedback' card lists only declines (src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:430-446); requested and approved keywords appear solely as counts in titleExtra (line 421-423) with no list and no action affordance. (2) The Hub's ADD_TO_STRATEGY action writes feedback='approved' + a tracked keyword but never touches keywordStrategy.siteKeywords/pageMap — inclusion waits for the next AI regeneration honoring the 'APPROVED KEYWORDS' prompt section (server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:344-346) while the Hub immediately labels the row IN_STRATEGY. There is also no staleness nudge anywhere that client feedback has accumulated since generatedAt, so the regeneration that actually closes the loop depends entirely on the admin remembering to run it. Net effect: a client requests a keyword, the platform records it, and nothing prompts or guarantees that anything happens.
findings.recommendation: List requested keywords on the strategy panel with a one-click action; either make ADD_TO_STRATEGY deterministically write the strategy artifact (page_keywords/siteKeywords) or relabel the lifecycle state honestly ('queued for next generation'); add a 'N feedback items since last generation — regenerate?' nudge reusing the existing localSync staleness pattern.

findings.title: Admin 'Site Target Keywords' volume/KD chips silently vanish after any new generation
findings.detail: server/routes/keyword-strategy.ts:253 destructures only the six arrays from assembleStoredKeywordStrategy and never re-attaches siteKeywordMetrics, while persistKeywordStrategy now writes the blob without it (table-strip) — verified: grep shows zero siteKeywordMetrics references in routes/keyword-strategy.ts, while the public route explicitly re-attaches it (server/routes/public-content.ts:138,165). The admin UI at src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:853 reads strategy.siteKeywordMetrics for the per-keyword volume/difficulty chips, so on any workspace generated post-strip the admin sees bare keyword chips with no metrics and no error — a paid-data display that silently degraded, and clients now see richer metrics than the admin does.
findings.recommendation: Re-attach assembled.siteKeywordMetrics in the admin GET serialization exactly as public-content.ts:165 does; fix the guard test that masks this by seeding the same metrics into both blob and table.

findings.title: Production Keyword Command Center shows fabricated filter counts on first paint
findings.detail: src/components/KeywordCommandCenter.tsx:186-211: until the deliberately deferred summary loads (150ms timer + fetch, lines 218-222), all 18 filter chip counts are computed from the currently visible 50-row page (placeholderCount filters `rows`, the paginated slice). Since all three Hub flags are OFF in production (shared/types/feature-flags.ts:37,44,49), this legacy shell IS the production keyword surface — every visit starts with factually wrong badge numbers (e.g. 'Needs Review: 2' when the true count is 40), which actively misleads the triage decision the chips exist to support.
findings.recommendation: Render count placeholders as '—' or a skeleton until summaryData arrives instead of page-local counts; or drop the 150ms defer (the summary endpoint is the same build the rows endpoint already runs).

findings.title: No way to preview the client portal as a given tier/config — admin flies blind on what each client actually sees
findings.detail: Client-facing visibility is controlled by scattered levers (seoClientView, analyticsClientView, clientPortalEnabled, tier, client-briefing-v2 flag, data-dependent nav items), and what a specific client sees varies along all of them. There is no 'view as' affordance: grep across WorkspaceSettings and layout finds none; the only admin → client-portal navigation is WorkspaceHome.tsx:465 jumping to the client ROI tab, and beta mode forces effectiveTier=premium. With two parallel overview implementations (legacy OverviewTab vs flag-gated Briefing suite) the admin must mentally simulate tier × flags × visibility toggles per workspace to know whether a client's Strategy tab is locked, which overview they get, or whether a tab disappeared on them.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Preview portal as this client' button on WorkspaceHome/WorkspaceSettings that opens the client route with an admin-only query override for tier and briefing flag (read-only), so a 30-second check replaces guesswork before client calls.

findings.title: Strategy panel: three regeneration affordances explained only by hover titles, and the competitors field cannot be cleared
findings.detail: src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:343-377 renders 'Update changed pages', 'Full refresh' (local/hybrid only), and 'Regenerate' side by side, plus a RefreshOrderingPrompt modal (line 388) — the cost/scope difference (incremental vs local-chain vs full, each metered against usage limits) is conveyed only via title attributes, so the admin risks burning a full generation when an incremental would do. Separately, the competitor-domains sync effect (lines 154-158) refills the input from workspaceData whenever it is empty (`if (...competitorDomains?.length && !competitors)`), so deleting all competitors and seeing the field repopulate is the literal behavior — the admin cannot clear saved competitors from this UI.
findings.recommendation: Collapse to one primary 'Regenerate' split-button whose menu explains scope and estimated credit cost per option; gate the competitor refill effect on a hasUserCleared ref (or only sync on workspace change) so an emptied field stays empty and saves as empty.

findings.title: Attributed dollar value — the most persuasive number outcome tracking computes — is invisible in the entire admin UI
findings.detail: Every scored outcome carries attributed_value (clicks delta × page CPC, migration 106), and getWinsWithValueByWorkspace exists server-side, but OutcomeTopWins shows only percentage deltas, the scorecard shows win rates, and the cross-workspace OutcomesOverview (src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomesOverview.tsx) aggregates actions/win-rate/attention with no dollar column — only the monthly client digest ever surfaces dollars. For an agency founder, 'value generated per workspace this quarter' is both the retention pitch and the prioritization signal for where to spend the next hour, and it is one query away from the screens that already exist. earlySignal (7-day on_track/no_movement) is similarly captured and broadcast but rendered nowhere, leaving pending actions a 30-day black box.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Value generated (90d)' column to OutcomesOverview rows and a dollar figure on Top Wins entries; show earlySignal as a small badge on pending actions in the feed.

analyses.dimension: ux-client: Are these features useful and usable for the client (non-technical business owner paying for SEO results)?
analyses.summary: The client portal's skeleton is genuinely strong — StrategyTab's feedback loops, the unified inbox, PostReviewCard's inline editing, and the #1-priority card are well above average for this product class, and the client-insight framing rules hold (zero purple in src/components/client/, narrative copy throughout). But the commercial core — proving the retainer is worth it — is materially under-delivered: the entire client outcome scorecard (~1,200 lines: OutcomeSummary, WeCalledIt, plus its public endpoint) was built and never mounted, dollar attribution computed per outcome is invisible to clients, the only live wins ledger sits behind a dark feature flag most clients don't have, and the wins that do render carry fabricated 'recommendation' text and a dead 'See full history' link. The habit loop is also broken at the feedback-closure point: clients who decline or request keywords get no signal of when it takes effect and no rank-progress trend on what they asked for. Several trust-eroding affordances ship today: a lock icon that opens an upgrade modal that cannot unlock the tab, a 'docx' download that delivers CSV, an 'ROI' badge that is actually a 0-100 score, and content recommendations (the highest-effort, highest-price items) that dead-end with no purchase path. The highest-leverage moves are all cheap: mount the already-built outcome scorecard, surface attributed dollar value, bridge content recs to the existing brief-purchase flow, and close the keyword-feedback loop with progress cards.

findings.title: Client proof-of-value scorecard fully built but never mounted (~1,200 lines dead)
findings.detail: src/components/client/OutcomeSummary.tsx (tiered win/loss scorecard with free/growth/premium views), WeCalledIt.tsx (month-grouped wins), FixRecommendations.tsx, ClientActionDetailModal.tsx, and InsightCards.tsx have zero importers (verified via repo-wide import grep). The backing endpoint GET /api/public/outcomes/:workspaceId/summary (server/routes/outcomes.ts:368) and hook useClientOutcomeSummary have no live UI consumer. The quantified 'what did our work achieve' answer — the whole commercial point of outcome tracking — never reaches the client on either overview layout. Meanwhile the only live outcome surface (WinsSurface) is gated behind the dark client-briefing-v2 flag, so most clients see no results ledger at all.
findings.recommendation: Mount OutcomeSummary (or a trimmed version) on the legacy OverviewTab or ROI tab now — the component, endpoint, hook, and WS invalidation keys all exist. Either wire WeCalledIt or delete it and the other dead files so the maintenance surface reflects reality.

findings.title: Clients never see the dollar value of work performed
findings.detail: action_outcomes.attributed_value (clicks delta x page CPC) is computed per scored outcome, but no client surface renders it: WinsSurface (src/components/client/Briefing/WinsSurface.tsx:74,86-88) shows raw metric deltas like 'clicks: +4.0 (+12.5%)' with jargon metric names (primary_metric rendered verbatim), and ROIDashboard shows generic traffic value, not work-attributed value. Only the monthly digest email uses the dollar figure. Additionally the wins endpoint fabricates the 'recommendation' field as '<action_type> action' (server/routes/outcomes.ts:395) instead of the real recommendation title, so the 'we recommended X and it worked' narrative isn't backed by what was actually recommended.
findings.recommendation: Add attributedValue to OutcomeWinEntry and render 'worth ~$X/mo' on win rows; join the original recommendation/action title into the wins projection instead of fabricating it from actionType. A 'value generated this quarter' stat (getWinsWithValueByWorkspace already exists server-side) is the single most retainer-justifying number available.

findings.title: Content recommendations dead-end with no purchase or request path
findings.detail: mapToProduct (server/recommendations.ts:879-901) has cases only for metadata/schema/accessibility/aeo/content_refresh; content_gap, keyword_gap, and topic_cluster recs hit the default and return {} — no productType, no price. In InsightsEngine.tsx the purchase CTA renders only when rec.productType exists (line 617), so the highest-effort, highest-price recommendations offer only 'I'll Handle This' (line 642). Yet the brief-purchase flow already exists two tabs away: StrategyContentOpportunitiesSection.tsx:194-205 opens the pricing modal and creates real paid content requests from content gaps.
findings.recommendation: Give content-type recs a 'Request this content' CTA that reuses the existing pricing-modal/content-request flow (source:'recommendation'), or at minimum deep-link to the matching Strategy content opportunity. This converts the engine's strongest suggestions into revenue instead of a shrug.

findings.title: Lock icon on Strategy tab opens an upgrade modal that cannot unlock it
findings.detail: clientDashboardNav.ts:28 sets strategyLocked = effectiveTier === 'free' || !ws.seoClientView — conflating tier gating with the admin visibility toggle. ClientHeader.tsx:223 routes any locked-tab click to setShowUpgradeModal(true). A paying Growth/Premium client whose admin disabled seoClientView sees a locked 'SEO Strategy' tab whose click pitches an upgrade that will not unlock anything — a genuinely misleading monetization affordance that can burn trust or trigger a wasted upgrade.
findings.recommendation: Split the two states: tier-locked → upgrade modal; admin-hidden → either omit the nav item entirely or show a neutral 'Your team manages this area' tooltip. One-line change in nav builder plus a branch in ClientHeader.

findings.title: Client keyword feedback gets no closure signal or progress loop
findings.detail: The feedback loop is real on the input side (declines/requests reach the next generation prompt) but invisible on the output side: (1) StrategyDeclinedKeywordsSection.tsx contains no messaging that declines apply only at the next admin-triggered regeneration (verified: zero matches for next/update/regenerate/applied), so a client who declines a keyword still sees it in the strategy indefinitely with no explanation; (2) client-requested/tracked keywords render with no rank trend — StrategyKeywordsSection.tsx has zero position/sparkline/trend rendering despite per-keyword rank_snapshots history existing and being rendered in the admin drawer. 'You asked for X — here's how it's moving' is the cheapest recurring reason to return to the dashboard and it doesn't exist.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'takes effect at the next strategy update' note (with last-generated date) to the declined section, and a small rank-trend chip on client-requested/tracked keywords reusing the existing rank-history endpoint. Both are display-only changes over data already served.

findings.title: The best client overview (briefing-v2) is dark for nearly all clients, leaving two divergent products
findings.detail: shared/types/feature-flags.ts:27 — 'client-briefing-v2': false by default. The magazine briefing (weekly opener, pulse strip, WinsSurface, RecommendedForYou) and the legacy OverviewTab+InsightsDigest (~1,270 lines) are maintained in parallel; WinsSurface — the only live outcome ledger — exists only in the v2 layout, and the weekly briefing cron only runs for flag-enabled paid workspaces, so the habit-loop content pipeline (weekly 'here's what happened' stories) is dark for most clients. The legacy OverviewTab is also overloaded: health score + 5 stat cards + up to 3 stacked banners + digest + intelligence card + predictions + insight feed + 3 sidebar cards competing on one screen.
findings.recommendation: Pick a cutover date for briefing-v2 (it is the better product for habit formation) or, if not ready, backport WinsSurface into the legacy overview so the wins ledger ships now. Avoid indefinitely maintaining two overview products under one URL.

findings.title: Opportunity Value claimed but never shown; mislabeled as 'ROI' where it does appear
findings.detail: InsightsEngine.tsx claims ranking 'prioritized by Opportunity Value' (lines 306, 421) but the file contains zero references to the opportunity field — list rows render only legacy high/medium/low impact + effort badges, so within-tier ordering looks arbitrary to the client. The only place OV surfaces is the Overview #1 card, where OverviewTab.tsx:286 renders Badge label='ROI {Math.round(topRec.opportunity.value)}' — labeling a 0-100 normalized score as ROI, a unit it is not. A client reading 'ROI 62' will reasonably assume 62% return or $62.
findings.recommendation: Render a small OV score (or the top component evidence string, already computed per rec) on each list row, and rename the badge to 'Priority score' or 'Opportunity 62/100'. The component breakdown bars on the #1 card are excellent — extend that pattern down the list.

findings.title: Free-tier inbox is nav-invisible but banner-reachable and unguarded
findings.detail: buildClientDashboardNav (clientDashboardNav.ts:42) includes Inbox only when isPaid, so free-tier clients have no Inbox nav item — yet ClientDashboard.tsx:698 renders InboxTab for any deep-linked tab with no tier guard, and OverviewTab.tsx:229-251 builds action-banner entries ('N SEO changes to review', briefs, posts, replies) that navigate free users into /inbox?tab=. Approvals sent to a free workspace are discoverable only via that one banner path; if the banner conditions aren't met, pending decisions are silently unreachable.
findings.recommendation: Decide the contract: if free workspaces can receive approvals, show the Inbox nav item (optionally with a count badge) for free tier; if they can't, block sending approvals to free workspaces admin-side. The current half-state strands client decisions.

findings.title: WinsSurface ships a dead link and a miscounted upsell teaser
findings.detail: src/components/client/Briefing/WinsSurface.tsx:135-144 — 'See full history →' is href='#' with title='Coming soon' and preventDefault, a visible dead-end in the flagship wins module. Line 160: the free-tier TierGate teaser reads '{wins.length} wins shipped this month' but wins is getTopWinsForWorkspace(ws, 10) (server/routes/outcomes.ts:388) — the all-time top 10, not this month — so the upsell hook makes a factually wrong claim that a skeptical owner can falsify.
findings.recommendation: Remove the link until history exists (or point it at the ROI tab), and either scope the teaser query to 30 days or change copy to 'X recent wins'. Honest numbers matter most in the upsell surface.

findings.title: Content Plan downloads deliver the wrong file format
findings.detail: ContentPlanTab.tsx:62-64 — handleDownload(format: 'docx' | 'pdf') opens /api/export/.../matrices?format=${format === 'docx' ? 'csv' : 'json'}. A client clicking 'Word doc' receives a CSV; clicking 'PDF' receives raw JSON, with no indication of the substitution. For a non-technical owner exporting the content plan to share internally, raw JSON is unusable and looks broken.
findings.recommendation: Either implement real docx/pdf export or relabel the buttons 'CSV' / 'JSON' to match what is delivered. The silent mismatch is worse than honest labels.

findings.title: Onboarding window: strategy empty state over-promises while the chat advisor disappears
findings.detail: StrategyTab.tsx:317-331 renders 'SEO strategy is being prepared... Check back soon' for any workspace where the admin simply never generated a strategy — a promise with no admin-side signal that a client is waiting (no notification, no activity entry). Simultaneously ClientChatWidget.tsx:90 returns null when overview, audit, and ga4Overview are all absent — i.e., during exactly the onboarding window when a new client most needs to ask questions, the SEO Advisor is gone entirely rather than degraded.
findings.recommendation: Have the empty state fire a lightweight client-waiting signal (activity/notification) the admin can see, and let the chat widget render in a limited 'getting set up' mode (it already has server-side grounding blocks independent of frontend chatDeps).

findings.title: Silent failures on client mutations in the recommendation engine
findings.detail: InsightsEngine.tsx:169 — regenerate failures are swallowed with an explicit '/* silently fail */' comment (button stops spinning, no message). Lines 191 and 202 — status update (mark in-progress/complete) and dismiss failures only console.error. A client clicks 'Mark Complete', the PATCH 400s/500s, and the UI gives no feedback; the optimistic cache write also doesn't update summary counts, so pill counts go stale until the WS round-trip. For the surface where clients triage their plan, failed actions looking like success undermines the whole decision loop.
findings.recommendation: Surface a toast on mutation failure (the toast infra exists in ClientDashboard) and reconcile summary counts in the optimistic update. Also consider a 'Dismissed' view with undo — dismiss is currently one-way in the UI even though the state machine allows un-dismiss.

findings.title: Normalized (lowercased) keyword strings leak into client-facing copy
findings.detail: upsertFeedback in server/keyword-command-center.ts (~3145-3156) stores keywordComparisonKey(keyword) — the canonicalized comparison key — as the keyword display string in keyword_feedback. Client-facing declined-keyword sections (StrategyDeclinedKeywordsSection) then render the lowercased/canonicalized text rather than the raw keyword the client or admin actually typed, degrading copy quality and violating the documented 'preserve raw display strings' rule in docs/rules/keyword-command-center.md.
findings.recommendation: Store the raw keyword in the keyword column and normalize only for comparison (the comparison-key helper already exists for lookups). Backfill is optional; new writes fixing forward is enough.

findings.title: Client-insight framing rules hold — protect them through the briefing-v2 cutover
findings.detail: Verified: zero purple-* classes in src/components/client/ (only comments referencing the rule), narrative outcome-oriented copy in WinsSurface/StrategyTab/InsightsEngine, TierGate used consistently, and the ?tab= deep-link contract correctly wired from the overview action banner into inbox sections (OverviewTab.tsx:250-251). The discipline is real. The main residual jargon leak is WinsSurface rendering entry.delta.primary_metric verbatim ('clicks', 'position', or worse for the broken metric names like 'click_recovery') — a metric-name-to-plain-English map would close it.
findings.recommendation: Add a small primaryMetric → plain-English label map in WinsSurface (mirroring the existing ACTION_LABELS pattern at WinsSurface.tsx:11-27), and keep the no-purple/narrative checks in pr-check coverage as briefing-v2 components multiply.

analyses.summary: The AI generation chain has genuinely strong bones: a typed operation registry (server/ai-operation-registry.ts) with per-op model/retry/research contracts, a unified callAI dispatcher that injects RESEARCH MODE grounding instructions, closed-set keyword-strategy prompts that hard-filter client-declined keywords and sanitize hallucinations against a candidate pool, and a deterministic (zero-AI) recommendation engine. The critical weakness is the learning layer that is supposed to make outputs improve over time: outcome scoring fabricates neutral/loss results for ~6 of 15 action types (metrics like 'click_recovery' exist in no snapshot), unexecuted suggestions and mislabeled backfill rows pollute win rates, and a 'difficulty' multiplier compares GSC position bins against provider keyword difficulty — yet these corrupted numbers are confidently asserted into strategy, brief, and recommendation prompts ('Content refreshes recover traffic X% of the time') and multiply recommendation rank scores. Second-order problems: the single highest-leverage call (master site synthesis) runs on gpt-5.4-mini at 3000 tokens while routine brief generation gets gpt-5.4 at 7000; client-paid request-driven briefs skip SERP/reference scraping entirely so their evidence ledger is empty; and the client SEO advisor's primary grounding is client-supplied unvalidated JSON rather than the server's own intelligence slices. Several places collect exactly the deterministic data (effort days, predicted EMV, rank snapshots, GA4 conversions) that would beat the current hardcoded priors, then never use it.

findings.title: Outcome scoring fabricates results for ~6 action types, then feeds the false 'evidence' into every AI generation prompt
findings.detail: server/outcome-scoring-defaults.ts assigns primary metrics that exist in no snapshot: content_refreshed→'click_recovery' (:28), internal_link_added→'target_improvement' (:32), brief_created→'content_produced' (:40). computeDelta (server/outcome-measurement.ts:188-198) coerces missing metrics to 0 vs 0 → every checkpoint scores 'neutral', so a content refresh can structurally never win. Worse, audit_fix_applied→'page_health_score' (:24) and voice_calibrated→'voice_score' (:44) exist in the baseline but fetchCurrentMetrics returns only GSC fields, so baseline 70 vs current 0 computes −100% → systematic 'loss'. The SEARCH_METRICS guards (outcome-measurement.ts:341-426) deliberately skip all sanity checks for non-search metrics, so nothing catches it. These fabricated scores flow into workspace_learnings, and formatLearningsSection (server/intelligence/formatters.ts:579) then asserts lines like 'Content refreshes recover traffic 0% of the time' with stated confidence into strategy synthesis (keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:359-366), brief generation, and recommendation context — corrupting the exact grounding the learnings contract (docs/rules/outcome-learning-default-path.md) exists to protect.
findings.recommendation: For each action type whose primary metric is not GSC-derivable, either (a) define a real computation (click_recovery = clicks delta vs pre-decay baseline; target_improvement = target page position delta) inside fetchCurrentMetrics, or (b) score 'inconclusive' when the metric is absent from the current snapshot — extend the SEARCH_METRICS guard to a generic 'metric present in both snapshots' guard in scoreActionAtCheckpoint. Add a unit test per action type asserting a known baseline/current pair produces the intended score.

findings.title: Learning loop contaminated twice over: unexecuted suggestions scored as executed, and backfill mislabels every recommendation as audit_fix_applied
findings.detail: (1) getPendingMeasurement (server/outcome-tracking.ts:58) selects 'WHERE measurement_complete = 0' with no attribution filter, so attribution='not_acted_on' suggestion rows (content-decay top-3, internal-link suggestions from webflow-analysis.ts) receive win/loss scores as if executed. computeWorkspaceLearnings likewise never filters attribution. (2) server/outcome-backfill.ts:197 hardcodes actionType:'audit_fix_applied' for every completed rec it backfills — and since auto-resolve/in-place-resolve completions never call recordAction, the backfill cron is the dominant path for platform-executed recs, so content_gap/keyword_gap/cannibalization outcomes are systematically logged under the wrong type. Both defects pollute winRateByActionType, which buildOutcomeAdjustment (server/outcome-learning-default-path.ts:54-63) turns into 0.75–1.25 rank-score multipliers applied to future recommendations (recommendations.ts sortRecommendations) and keyword candidates (keyword-recommendations.ts:200-214). The calibration signal is poisoned at the source.
findings.recommendation: Add `AND attribution != 'not_acted_on'` to getPendingMeasurement and filter attribution in computeWorkspaceLearnings (or only score not_acted_on rows after external-detection promotes them). In outcome-backfill.ts, map rec type through the existing recommendationOutcomeActionType mapping (server/recommendations.ts:198-242) instead of the hardcoded literal — the compile-time-exhaustive mapping already exists for exactly this purpose.

findings.title: Difficulty win-rate multiplier compares incommensurate quantities (GSC position bins vs provider keyword difficulty)
findings.detail: computeStrategyLearnings bins 'winRateByDifficultyRange' from baseline GSC *position* (server/workspace-learnings.ts:216-226: pos≥51→'0-20', pos≤10→'81-100'), explicitly labeled an approximation. But buildOutcomeAdjustment (server/outcome-learning-default-path.ts:65-79) looks up real provider keyword difficulty (0-100 KD from DataForSEO/SEMRush) against those position-derived bins and multiplies candidate scores by up to ±12%. A KD-30 keyword is matched against the win rate of keywords that happened to start at position 31-50 — unrelated populations. Adjacent defects in the same module: winRateByFormat keys are sourceTypes ('post','insight'), not content formats (workspace-learnings.ts:119-131), yet formatters.ts:576 renders them as '<format> outperforms <format>'; optimalWordCount is a permanent null placeholder (:167); keywordVolumeSweetSpot is just min/max impressions of wins. AI prompts receive all of these as confident workspace-specific learnings.
findings.recommendation: Store the actual provider difficulty on tracked_actions at record time (it's available at every keyword-bearing call site) and bin on it; until then, remove the difficulty multiplier from buildOutcomeAdjustment and the 'Keywords with difficulty X have highest win rate' prompt line — a wrong calibration is worse than none. Rename winRateByFormat to winRateBySource or derive real format from brief pageType.

findings.title: The platform's highest-leverage AI call (master site synthesis) runs on the cheapest model at 3000 maxTokens
findings.detail: keyword-site-synthesis — the single call that produces siteKeywords, opportunities, contentGaps, and quickWins for the strategy that grounds nearly every other surface — defaults to gpt-5.4-mini (server/ai-operation-registry.ts:295) and is invoked with maxTokens 3000 (server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:1379,1386). Meanwhile routine content-brief generation gets gpt-5.4 at 7000 tokens, and even content-post-seo-meta (a metadata one-liner) gets gpt-5.4 (registry :116). CLAUDE.md's own model policy reserves gpt-5.4 for 'higher-quality ops'. The per-page batch calls reasonably use mini (N parallel calls, closed-set selection), but the master synthesis is one call per generation, sits behind a Zod safeParse with a single schema-feedback retry, and its failures degrade to typed-empty + deterministic backfill — i.e., quality silently falls back to the backfill floor. The cost delta of upgrading one call per strategy generation is trivial relative to the DataForSEO credits already spent assembling its keyword universe.
findings.recommendation: Change keyword-site-synthesis defaultModel to gpt-5.4 (and consider 4000-5000 maxTokens to reduce truncation-induced schema retries). Persist the generationQuality telemetry per run (currently log-only at keyword-strategy-generation.ts:602) so the before/after effect of the model change is measurable.

findings.title: Client-paid request-driven briefs get strictly weaker grounding than free-form admin briefs — and an empty evidence ledger downstream
findings.detail: generateStandaloneBrief scrapes reference URLs, live SERP data (PAA + top results), and top-GA4 style-example pages (server/content-brief-generation-job.ts:114-142). generateBriefForRequest (:203-308) — the path used for every client-submitted/purchased content request — performs none of these: it passes only GSC related queries, provider keyword metrics, GA4 page performance, and decay context into generateBrief. No realPeopleAlsoAsk/realTopResults/referenceUrls are persisted, so when the resulting post is AI-reviewed, buildReviewEvidence finds nothing and the claim-verification ledger degrades to 'no saved source pack'. The content clients pay for is generated with less factual grounding and is less verifiable than ad-hoc admin briefs, inverting the value proposition.
findings.recommendation: Call scrapeSerpData(request.targetKeyword) and (when a target page or strategy card supplies reference URLs) scrapeUrls in generateBriefForRequest, mirroring the standalone path — it is already a background job, so the added latency is acceptable. Both paths should share one enrichment helper so they cannot drift again.

findings.title: Client SEO advisor is grounded on client-supplied unvalidated JSON instead of server intelligence slices
findings.detail: POST /api/public/search-chat validates context as z.record(z.unknown()) with no size cap (server/routes/public-analytics.ts:302) and serializes it verbatim into the system prompt: 'Current data context:\n${JSON.stringify(context, null, 2)}' (:509). The data-inventory booleans (hasSearch/hasStrategy/etc., :337-344) are also derived from this client payload. So the AI's primary factual grounding for 'their data' is whatever the browser sends — a prompt-injection vector (the system prompt's anti-override rules at :495 are themselves positioned above attacker-controlled JSON), a fabricated-data vector ('the AI asserted my traffic tripled'), and an unbounded token-cost vector since the frontend re-serializes the entire dashboard dataset every message. The server already possesses authoritative versions of all of this via buildWorkspaceIntelligence/buildSeoPromptContext (used only for supplementary blocks in the same handler).
findings.recommendation: Replace the client-supplied context with server-side assembly: derive the data blocks from intelligence slices + the same public-endpoint readers the dashboard uses, keyed by workspaceId. If a client-side hint is needed (active tab, date range), accept a small enum-validated object with a strict size cap, never freeform JSON injected into the prompt.

findings.title: AI post review and voice scoring evaluate only the first 8000 characters with no disclosure, and the review call bypasses the operation registry
findings.detail: The ai-review route truncates the stripped post text to 8000 chars (server/routes/content-posts.ts:522-523) before asking for brand_voice/internal_links/meta verdicts — for an 1800+ word post the tail is never reviewed, and claimsToVerify ARE extracted from the full text (:524) while the model only sees the head, so flagged claims may not even appear in its input. scoreVoiceMatch has the same 8000-char cut (server/content-posts-ai.ts:799-800). Neither result discloses partial coverage to the admin. The review call also uses feature:'content-review' with inline model/params (:557-566) rather than a registered operation, despite returning schema-validated JSON — exactly the 'reusable/high-value structured output' case the ai-operation-contracts rule says must be registered.
findings.recommendation: Chunk the review: run checklist items that are inherently whole-document (internal_links presence, word count) deterministically (regex/count — no AI needed), and review prose-quality items per section or over a larger window. Surface 'reviewed N of M words' in the response. Register 'content-review' in server/ai-operation-registry.ts.

findings.title: ai-fix for factual issues blindly rewrites sections[0] and never receives the extracted claims; internal_links fallback explicitly invites fabricated URLs
findings.detail: For factual_accuracy/no_hallucinations the fix targets post.sections[0] unconditionally (server/routes/content-posts.ts:794) regardless of where the flagged claim lives, and the claimsToVerify list produced by the review/evidence ledger is never threaded into the fix prompt (:800-807) — the fix and the verification system are disconnected, so the model 'conservatively rewrites' a section that may contain no flagged claim while the real one survives. Separately, the internal_links fix prompt falls back to 'Use a plausible internal link like /blog or /services' when the brief has no suggestions (:722) — a direct instruction to fabricate a URL, contradicting RESEARCH_MODE_INSTRUCTIONS ('Do not invent... source URLs', server/ai.ts:49-57) on a route that otherwise sets researchMode:false anyway.
findings.recommendation: Pass the flagged claim text into the factual fix prompt and locate the target section by searching sections for the claim substring (fall back to asking the admin to pick). For internal_links, when no suggestions exist either pull real paths from the pageMap (available via the seoContext slice) or return an error telling the admin to add suggestions — never instruct the model to invent a path.

findings.title: Evidence ledger matches claims against titles/URLs only because scraped page text is discarded
findings.detail: server/content-review-evidence-ledger.ts builds claim-evidence candidates by token overlap between the claim and `${result.title} ${result.url}` for SERP top results (buildReferenceCandidates) and the PAA question strings — a 2-token overlap on ≥3-char words yields a 'possible' match and 3 tokens yields 'strong'. The actual page content scraped at brief time (ScrapedPage bodies fetched by scrapeUrls/scrapeSerpData) is used once in the brief prompt and never persisted, so the 'deterministic evidence' backing the human factual-accuracy review is essentially topical-title similarity, not source text containing the statistic. A claim like '72% of marketers...' gets paired with any result whose title shares two topic words. This undersells an otherwise honest design (force-failed provenance items, human review) by giving reviewers weak pointers.
findings.recommendation: Persist scraped source text (or a per-source extracted-numbers index) alongside realTopResults on the brief, and match numeric claims against source body text. Even storing only sentences containing numeric tokens per source would make 'strong' matches mean something.

findings.title: Recommendation engine is blind to the content pipeline and discards the prompt context it pays to build
findings.detail: generateRecommendations overrides the builder's default slices to ['seoContext','clientSignals','learnings'] (server/recommendations.ts:1091), dropping contentPipeline which buildRecommendationGenerationContext includes by default precisely so consumers can see in-flight briefs/posts (server/intelligence/generation-context-builders.ts:139). Result: content_gap/keyword_gap/topic_cluster recommendations can duplicate work already being produced — a brief in client_review for the same keyword does not suppress the rec. Additionally the builder always runs formatForPrompt (:75-80) and the engine, being deterministic, never reads promptContext — wasted token-formatting work on every regen (debounced regens fire after every strategy persist, outcome measurement pass, and opportunity event).
findings.recommendation: Add 'contentPipeline' to the slice list and suppress (or down-rank with an 'in progress' annotation) content-creation recs whose target keyword matches an active brief/post/request — content-pipeline-slice already computes coverage gaps, so the inverse check is cheap. Add a skipPromptFormatting option to buildGenerationContext for deterministic consumers.

findings.title: OV scoring runs on hardcoded effort priors and a win-rate proxy while the real calibration data (time-to-completion, predicted EMV) is collected and ignored
findings.detail: computeOpportunityValue divides by DEFAULT_EFFORT_DAYS per branch (server/scoring/opportunity-value.ts:41,346) — fixed priors the file's own comments say should be derived from outcomes ('P5 derives real time-to-implement'). updatedAt−createdAt on completed recs is exactly that dataset and is never aggregated. Similarly predicted_emv (migration 116) accrues on manual completions but ov-calibration (server/scoring/ov-calibration.ts) still uses a score-weighted win-rate proxy, and platform-executed completions get predictedEmv:null via backfill, so the realized-vs-predicted dataset accrues mostly from the rarest path. Net effect: the ranking spine clients see ('#1 priority') is calibrated by priors plus a multiplier polluted by the finding-2 contamination, while the honest calibration inputs sit unused. This is a place where deterministic computation strictly beats the current heuristics and no AI is involved at all.
findings.recommendation: Aggregate median time-to-completion per rec branch per workspace (fall back to platform-wide, then DEFAULT_EFFORT_DAYS) and feed it into effortDays. Snapshot predictedEmv on ALL completion paths (auto-resolve at recommendations.ts:2295 and in-place resolve have the rec object in hand) so the P6 realized-vs-predicted calibration has data when it ships.

findings.title: Strategy generation is outcome-invisible after the first run and its quality telemetry is log-only — the system cannot learn whether strategies work
findings.detail: persistKeywordStrategy records a strategy_keyword_added tracked action only when none exists ('if (!getActionBySource(\'strategy\', ws.id))', server/keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:184), with pageUrl null — so regenerations, the events whose impact matters, leave no outcome trail, and the one action that exists scores permanently inconclusive (position metric, no page). Consequently learningsDomain:'strategy' (injected into the synthesis prompt at keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:296-373) is structurally starved: the strategy prompt's 'learnings' block can essentially never contain strategy-specific evidence. Meanwhile GenerationQuality (pool size, suppressed count, backfill floor hits) is computed then only logged (server/keyword-strategy-generation.ts:588-602), and strategy_history retains 5 snapshots that nothing but the latest-diff reads — so neither the AI nor the founder can see whether generation quality or past strategy choices regressed.
findings.recommendation: Record per-keyword tracked actions for net-new pageMap primary keywords on each generation (page URL + keyword baseline exist at persist time; rank_snapshots provide the measurement), remove the once-ever guard, and persist GenerationQuality rows (workspace, generatedAt, poolSize, suppressed, backfilled, floorHit) so a cross-workspace generation-health view and eval fixtures fall out for free.

findings.title: Strategy prompts receive aggregate learnings but no per-keyword movement evidence, and conversion data is fetched per generation then thrown away
findings.detail: The synthesis prompt assembly (server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts:296-373) is the best-grounded in the platform — slices, declined/requested/approved keywords, gap votes, coverage gaps, local evidence — but it never receives 'what happened since the last strategy': per-keyword rank deltas from rank_snapshots (daily GSC, used only for the Hub drawer sparkline), tracked-keyword baselines (written at track time, consumed by nothing), or the prior strategy snapshot. The model re-derives priorities each run with no memory of which prior picks moved.
