Similarly, ga4Conversions/ga4EventsByPage are fetched for every generation (keyword-strategy-search-data.ts) and injected as prompt text, but conversion-weighted quick-win ranking is otherwise discarded — quick-win roiScore could be computed deterministically from clicks×CPC×conversion-rate rather than left to the model's judgment.
findings.recommendation: Inject a compact deterministic block: 'SINCE LAST GENERATION: keyword X pos 14→8 (improving), keyword Y pos 6→19 (declined)' built from rank_snapshots diffed against the previous strategy's pageMap, capped at ~20 movers. Compute quick-win ordering deterministically (position-to-CTR curve already exists in server/scoring/ctr-curve.ts) and let the AI only write rationale.

findings.title: LearningsSlice 'disabled' availability is a dead union member — the documented per-workspace disable contract does not exist
findings.detail: buildOutcomeLearningStatusNote has a 'disabled' branch with bespoke prompt copy (server/outcome-learning-default-path.ts:100-101), and docs/rules/outcome-learning-default-path.md implies a per-workspace disable switch, but assembleLearnings never produces availability:'disabled' — the branch is unreachable and there is no actual mechanism to exclude a workspace's (currently corrupted, per findings 1-3) learnings from generation prompts. Given the contamination upstream, the missing kill-switch is the one availability state that would currently be most useful: there is no way to tell brief/strategy/rec generation 'ignore measured outcomes for this workspace' while the scoring defects are remediated.
findings.recommendation: Either implement the disable switch (a workspaces flag read by assembleLearnings) or delete the dead union member and prompt branch. Implementing it is preferable: it doubles as the remediation lever for the scoring-contamination findings.

analyses.dimension: gaps — what is missing that should be considered
analyses.summary: The platform's biggest absences cluster in three areas. First, measurement depth: rank tracking is GSC-only (server/rank-tracking-scheduler.ts imports only getSearchOverview/getSearchQueryObservations), so keywords with zero impressions are invisible, true SERP positions and competitor positions are never observed, and competitor/backlink/AI-search visibility all lack any time-series — table stakes for Ahrefs/Semrush-calibrated expectations. Second, proof-of-value delivery: the system computes attributed dollar values, win scorecards, and per-keyword baselines, but the client scorecard is dead code (src/components/client/OutcomeSummary.tsx, zero importers), dollar attribution renders nowhere, and keyword-level outcomes are never scored — the commercial heart of an agency platform is built server-side and unshipped client-side. Third, learning loops: published-content performance is never joined back to the brief attributes that produced it, no Clearscope-style term-coverage grading exists, 12-month volume trend arrays sit unused for seasonality, and there is no goals/forecast framing for clients. The highest value-to-effort items for a solo founder are mounting the already-built outcome scorecard, an alert/insight on lost visibility (detection already runs daily), and a client-requested-keyword progress card — all are mostly wiring over existing data.

findings.title: Rank tracking is GSC-impressions-only — no true SERP position checks for national keywords
findings.detail: server/rank-tracking-scheduler.ts pulls positions exclusively from GSC (imports only getSearchOverview/getSearchQueryObservations from server/search-console.ts at lines 12-13, 5000-query cap); grep confirms zero SERP-API calls in server/rank-tracking*.ts. Consequences: (1) a keyword with no impressions (not yet ranking, or ranking page 5+) shows no position ever — yet clients can add tracked keywords via the public API (server/routes/public-content.ts:631) and the Strategy tab implies a trend will appear, a soft-broken promise for exactly the aspirational keywords clients request; (2) GSC 'position' is an impression-weighted average, not an observed SERP rank; (3) no competitor positions per tracked keyword, no SERP-feature presence over time. The DataForSEO SERP organic endpoint is ALREADY integrated for local SEO (server/local-seo.ts:111 holds full serp/google/organic/live/advanced responses), so the provider plumbing exists.
findings.recommendation: Add an optional provider-backed SERP check for tracked keywords (weekly cadence, budget-capped via the existing recordPaidCall pattern) that stores observed rank + top-10 domains into rank_snapshots alongside GSC data. Prioritize keywords with zero GSC impressions and client-requested keywords. Reuse the local-seo SERP fetch path and the existing per-workspace provider-credit ceiling from keyword-strategy-universe.

findings.title: Competitor tracking is point-in-time only — no time series, share-of-voice, or movement alerts
findings.detail: Every competitor artifact is a snapshot: /api/seo/competitive-intel (server/routes/seo-provider.ts:50) is a live provider read with a 48h frontend cache (src/components/strategy/CompetitiveIntel.tsx:97-99); keyword_gaps rows store competitor_position only as of the last strategy generation (migration 088); workspace_authority is a single-row latest capture (migration 108, PRIMARY KEY workspace_id — no history); competitorKeywordData in the strategy blob (≤150 rows) has zero UI consumers. The outcome-tracking competitorContext/CompetitorMovement types exist in shared types and Zod schemas but NOTHING populates them (only server/schemas/outcome-schemas.ts references them). There is no 'competitor overtook you on keyword X' alert, no competitor new-content detection, no share-of-voice metric — all standard in Semrush/Ahrefs position tracking and in monthly agency reports.
findings.recommendation: Persist competitor positions per tracked keyword when the SERP check (previous finding) runs — top-10 domains are free in the same SERP response. Derive: (a) a share-of-voice trend for the client dashboard, (b) opportunity_events when a named competitor passes the client (the opportunity-events ledger from migration 110 already exists), (c) populate competitorContext on outcomes so wins/losses can be explained. Start with competitors already stored in workspaces.competitor_domains.

findings.title: Zero AI-search visibility tracking (AI Overviews, ChatGPT/LLM mentions) despite shipping llms.txt
findings.detail: grep for ai_overview/aiOverview/llm_mention across server/ and src/ returns nothing. The platform generates llms.txt for every workspace (queued in server/keyword-strategy-follow-ons.ts:74) — i.e. it invests in being legible to AI crawlers — but never measures whether the client appears in AI Overviews or LLM answers. SERP-feature data captured per keyword (server/providers/dataforseo-provider.ts:598-615 maps serp_item_types) could already flag ai_overview presence, and DataForSEO exposes LLM-mentions endpoints. In 2026 'do we show up in ChatGPT/AI Overviews' is the question agency clients ask first; competitors (Semrush AI toolkit, Ahrefs Brand Radar) ship this.
findings.recommendation: Phase 1 (cheap): when SERP features are fetched during strategy enrichment, persist whether ai_overview is present for each strategy keyword and whether the client's domain is cited — surface as a strategy section and client briefing story. Phase 2: a monthly DataForSEO LLM-mentions pull for the brand + top keywords feeding a 'AI visibility' card. This also closes the loop on the llms.txt investment.

findings.title: No backlink monitoring — single live overview read, no new/lost tracking or link recommendations
findings.detail: Backlink data enters the platform only as a live getBacklinksOverview call inside the seoContext slice (server/intelligence/seo-context-slice.ts:285-317) and the one-row workspace_authority capture used for OV authority strength (migration 108). There is no backlink history table, no new/lost referring-domain detection, no toxic-link flagging, and the recommendation engine never mints link-building recommendations — the BacklinkProfile strategy card is a read-only snapshot. New/lost backlink alerts and link-gap analysis are core Ahrefs/Semrush features and a standard line in agency monthly reports; the SeoDataProvider interface already abstracts the data source (server/seo-data-provider.ts:138).
findings.recommendation: Add a weekly backlink snapshot row (referring domains, total backlinks, authority bucket) per workspace — the fetch already happens for the slice, so persist it instead of discarding. Derive a simple 'referring domains trend' card for the client ROI/health tab and an opportunity_event on significant losses. Defer full link-prospecting; the trend + alert covers 80% of client-report value.

findings.title: Client proof-of-value scorecard fully built but never mounted — dollar attribution invisible everywhere
findings.detail: Verified: src/components/client/OutcomeSummary.tsx (complete tiered scorecard) and WeCalledIt.tsx have zero importers (only src/hooks/client/useClientOutcomes.ts:14 references the hook); GET /api/public/outcomes/:id/summary (server/routes/outcomes.ts:368) has no live consumer. Meanwhile attributed_value (real dollars per outcome, migration 106) renders nowhere in admin OR client UI — Top Wins shows percentages only — and the archive tables hold 24 months of history with no read path. The commercial core of outcome tracking ('here is what our work earned you') is computed server-side and undelivered. For an agency platform this is the retention feature.
findings.recommendation: Mount OutcomeSummary on the client ROI or Overview tab (the endpoint and component exist — this is wiring, not building), add a 'value generated this quarter' stat using the existing getWinsWithValueByWorkspace query to admin OutcomeTopWins and the client scorecard, and fix the fabricated OutcomeWinEntry.recommendation string (server/routes/outcomes.ts:395) to carry the real recommendation title first so the framing is honest. Note: upstream scoring bugs (broken metrics for ~6 action types) should be fixed before showing clients more numbers.

findings.title: Keyword-level outcome attribution missing — baselines captured, never scored; client-requested keywords get no progress loop
findings.detail: TrackedKeyword rows store baselinePosition/baselineClicks/baselineImpressions at track-time, and rank_snapshots accumulate daily per-keyword history, but no outcome module consumes either: server/outcome-measurement.ts measures only via GSC page trends + page_keywords, no Hub lifecycle action creates a tracked_action, and 'did promoting this keyword pay off' is structurally unanswerable. The client-facing half is the bigger miss: clients request keywords via /api/public/keyword-feedback (CLIENT_REQUESTED source) and approval auto-creates a tracked keyword (server/routes/public-portal.ts:354-360), but nothing ever shows them 'you asked for X — here is its rank since we started': the data for a high-retention 'your requests, your results' card already exists end-to-end.
findings.recommendation: Two slices: (1) a keyword-outcome bridge — when a keyword is promoted/tracked, record a tracked_action whose measurement reads rank_snapshots (position delta from the stored baseline) instead of page trends; (2) a client Strategy-tab card listing client-requested keywords with their rank sparkline from the existing /history endpoint. (2) alone is small and directly visible to paying clients.

findings.title: Published-content performance never feeds back into content generation — the 'what content works here' dataset is unexploited
findings.detail: Every published post gets a GSC baseline (server/routes/content-publish.ts:179-193) and content_posts stores the producing brief's attributes (page type, target keyword, word-count target, generationStyle), but no join exists: post-level performance is never correlated with brief attributes, generationStyle outcomes (approval rate, client-edit volume, post-publish clicks) are never compared, voiceScore is never aggregated, titleVariants/metaDescVariants are generated on every brief with no variant pick or CTR follow-up (pure token spend), and content_post_versions trigger metadata (client_edit vs regenerate_section — a direct signal of where AI drafts fail) is unanalyzed. Clearscope/agency tooling at minimum reports per-content performance; here the learnings slice only carries coarse win rates whose 'format' keys are actually sourceTypes (server/workspace-learnings.ts).
findings.recommendation: Start with a per-post performance view: join content_posts.publishedAt + targetPageSlug to the existing GSC page-trend reads and show clicks/position trend per published post on the admin Posts tab and client ContentTab. Then add a quarterly aggregation (win rate by page type / word-count band / generationStyle) into workspace_learnings so brief generation can cite it. The version-trigger edit-rate metric is one SQL query and would immediately show which section types clients rewrite.

findings.title: No Clearscope/Surfer-style draft optimization grading against live SERP terms
findings.detail: The post pipeline has a unify pass with keyword-density caps and a 6-item review checklist, but no term-coverage scoring: drafts are never graded against the vocabulary/entities of current top-10 results for the target keyword. SERP scraping exists (scrapeSerpData on the standalone brief path) but the scraped page text is discarded after brief generation (only title/URL token overlap survives into the evidence ledger), and the request-driven path — the one clients pay through — never scrapes at all (server/content-brief-generation-job.ts:203-297). A content-optimization score is the headline feature of Clearscope/SurferSEO/MarketMuse and the main reason agencies pay for a second tool alongside their writer.
findings.recommendation: Persist scraped SERP page text at brief time (also fixes the evidence-ledger gap), extract a weighted term list deterministically (TF-IDF over top results — no AI call needed), store it on the brief, and add a coverage score to ReviewChecklist that recomputes as the admin edits. Extend SERP scraping to the request-driven path first since that is the paid path.

findings.title: Lost-visibility and rank-drop events detected daily but never alert anyone
findings.detail: detectLostVisibility runs in the daily rank-tracking scheduler and writes discovered_queries rows (server/client-discovered-queries.ts), but the only consumer is the passive LOST_VISIBILITY filter chip in the admin Keyword Hub — no insight is minted, no recommendation, no email, no client-facing story. Contrast: GSC traffic anomalies DO email the team (notifyAnomalyAlert wired at server/search-console.ts:566, admin recipient at server/email.ts:384-399). 'A query you used to rank for disappeared' is precisely the proactive-alert moment that justifies a monthly retainer, and the platform already has the insight pipeline, briefing story templates, and email queue to deliver it.
findings.recommendation: When detectLostVisibility fires for a query above an impressions threshold, mint an analytics insight (the InsightType registration pattern is documented) and an opportunity_event so the recommendation regen picks it up; include it in the weekly briefing story candidates. This is wiring three existing systems together, not new detection.

findings.title: Seasonality intelligence is free and unused — 12-month volume trends cached per keyword, never consumed
findings.detail: keyword_metrics_cache stores a 12-point monthly volume trend array for every cached keyword (server/keyword-metrics-cache.ts:23,80,145 — populated from DataForSEO monthly_searches at server/providers/dataforseo-provider.ts:531,656,868) but grep shows no renderer or consumer of the trend field anywhere. Outcome actions are stamped with seasonalTag (month/quarter) that no learnings dimension uses. Result: no 'publish this in March, search peaks in May' timing guidance in briefs, no seasonal sort in the Keyword Hub, no content-calendar awareness — a standard Semrush/Ahrefs keyword-detail feature the platform has already paid provider credits to collect.
findings.recommendation: Surface the cached trend as a sparkline in the KeywordDetailDrawer (data is already in the row's metrics path), and add a deterministic 'seasonal peak month' derivation injected into brief generation and the content Planner so topic scheduling can front-run peaks. Zero new API spend.

findings.title: No goals/targets or forecast framing for clients — 'are we on track?' is unanswerable
findings.detail: grep for trafficGoal/targetTraffic/goal_ across server/ and shared/ returns nothing — there is no concept of a client target (traffic, leads, rankings) anywhere in the data model. The OV engine computes emvPerWeek predictions and predicted_emv accrues per completed action (migration 116), so forward-looking machinery exists, but clients never see a trajectory ('current trend vs. where you'll be in 6 months if recommendations land') or a goal line on any chart. Monthly agency reporting universally anchors on goals; without them the dashboard reports activity, not progress, and the monthly digest/briefing have no 'on track / behind' narrative spine.
findings.recommendation: Add a minimal workspace-level goal (target organic clicks/mo + date) settable by admin or client (BrandTab/business-priorities pattern already exists for client-editable workspace fields), draw it as a reference line on the Performance tab chart, and have the briefing/digest compare trajectory vs. goal using the existing period-comparison reads. Defer ML forecasting — a linear trend vs. goal line delivers most of the perceived value.

findings.title: Strategy evolution is invisible — 5 history snapshots retained but only the latest is ever read
findings.detail: strategy_history stores 5 snapshots per workspace inside the persist transaction (server/keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:176-179) but only the latest is consumed (diff route at server/routes/keyword-strategy.ts:303; ux builder latestHistory) — 4 of 5 snapshots are write-only. No surface (admin, client, or AI slice) can answer 'how has the strategy evolved — keyword count, coverage, average position across generations — and did past changes work?'. Combined with the once-ever strategy_keyword_added outcome guard (server/keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:184), strategy effectiveness over time is structurally unmeasurable, even though the snapshots and generationQuality telemetry (computed then only logged at server/keyword-strategy-generation.ts:588-602) contain exactly the needed data.
findings.recommendation: Add a compact 'strategy over time' card on the admin Analysis tab: per-snapshot keyword count, pages mapped, avg position, gaps closed — derived from the already-stored history rows. Persist generationQuality per generation (one small table) at the same time so generation-health regressions become visible. Feed a one-line trend into the seoContext slice so AdminChat can reason about strategy trajectory.

findings.title: Local SEO lacks Google Business Profile integration — reviews and GBP signals invisible
findings.detail: The local SEO subsystem measures visibility via DataForSEO SERP scraping (server/local-seo.ts, local-pack detection at :1840,1925) and stores client locations (migration 099), but grep confirms no Google Business Profile API integration anywhere: no review count/rating monitoring, no review-response workflow, no GBP post publishing, no GBP insights (calls/direction requests). For local-SEO clients — the segment the local posture targets — reviews are the #1 local ranking factor and the first thing they expect on a monthly report; every local-SEO platform (BrightLocal, Semrush Local) leads with it.
findings.recommendation: Lowest-cost first step: the local-pack SERP responses the platform already fetches include competitor rating/review counts — persist the client's own rating/review-count per market from those responses and trend it on the local visibility surface, with an opportunity_event on rating drops. Full GBP API integration (OAuth, review replies) is a later, larger investment; flag it on the roadmap rather than building now.

analyses.summary: The platform collects far more data than it exploits. The single biggest pattern is "computed/persisted but never consumed": dollar attribution per outcome, predicted-vs-realized EMV pairs, 7-day early signals, strategy history snapshots, searchSignals blobs, and generation-quality telemetry all exist in the database today with zero or one consumer. A second pattern is finished-but-unmounted client proof-of-value surfaces (OutcomeSummary scorecard, its live public endpoint) — the commercial payoff of outcome tracking is built and dark. Third, the richest per-keyword dataset (KCC lifecycle states, value scores, rank histories, client-requested keyword provenance) is invisible to both AI consumers and clients. Fourth, no cross-workspace aggregation exists anywhere except the keyword-metrics cache, even though anonymized win-rate priors would solve the cold-start problem the learnings-availability contract explicitly papers over. Most unlocks here are S/M effort because the hard half (collection, schema, endpoints) is already shipped.

findings.title: Dollar attribution (attributed_value) is computed per outcome but rendered almost nowhere
findings.detail: outcome-measurement computes attributedValue (clicks delta x page CPC) for every scored outcome and stores it in action_outcomes.attributed_value (migration 106). A ready-made query getWinsWithValueByWorkspace exists (server/outcome-tracking.ts:73,552). Verified the ONLY UI consumer in the entire frontend is src/components/client/MonthlyDigest.tsx:115-116 (a small caption line). The admin Top Wins tab (src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomeTopWins.tsx) shows percentage deltas only; clients never see dollars on WinsSurface or anywhere else. The most persuasive retention/sales number the platform computes — 'value generated this quarter' — is invisible.
findings.recommendation: Surface attributed_value in three places: (1) admin OutcomeTopWins rows and the OutcomeScorecard header ('~$X attributed this quarter', one call to getWinsWithValueByWorkspace); (2) client WinsSurface / ROI tab via the existing /api/public/outcomes/:id/wins payload (OutcomeWinEntry already carries the data path); (3) a per-workspace rollup field on LearningsSlice so AdminChat and the monthly digest can cite it. No new collection needed.

findings.title: Client outcome scorecard is fully built (component + live endpoint) but never mounted
findings.detail: Verified src/components/client/OutcomeSummary.tsx (a complete tiered free/growth/premium scorecard) is imported by zero components — the only references to useClientOutcomeSummary are the dead component itself and its hook definition (src/hooks/client/useClientOutcomes.ts:14). The server endpoint GET /api/public/outcomes/:workspaceId/summary (server/routes/outcomes.ts:368) is live and tier-safe. The quantified 'what did our work achieve' answer — the commercial point of the entire outcome-tracking subsystem — reaches clients on neither the legacy overview nor briefing-v2.
findings.recommendation: Mount OutcomeSummary on the client Overview or ROI tab (one import + placement decision). Before mounting, fix the upstream scoring honesty issues (not_acted_on rows polluting win rates per workspace-learnings.ts:392-409) so the scorecard shown to clients is truthful. This is days-not-weeks because everything below the JSX is already shipped.

findings.title: No cross-workspace aggregation exists; anonymized win-rate priors would fix the learnings cold-start
findings.detail: Every learnings/calibration read is workspace-scoped: workspace_learnings, computeOvCalibration (server/scoring/ov-calibration.ts), buildOutcomeAdjustment (server/outcome-learning-default-path.ts) all fall back to neutral multipliers / availability='no_data' for young workspaces — which is most of them, since outcomes need 30-90 days to score. Meanwhile the platform already has tracked_actions + action_outcomes across all workspaces and even runs cross-workspace loops (outcome-playbooks.ts:128 weekly cron iterates every workspace; routes/outcomes.ts overview does too). The only cross-workspace data structure in the codebase is keyword_metrics_cache (migration 025, global by design via server/keyword-metrics-cache.ts) — proving the pattern is acceptable. No anonymized benchmark ('across our portfolio, content refreshes win 41% of the time; CTR fixes pay off in 30 days') exists for AI grounding, client trust copy, or cold-start score priors.
findings.recommendation: Add a platform_learnings aggregate (single global row or table keyed by action_type/difficulty bucket) recomputed by the existing weekly cron, containing anonymized win rates, median days-to-result, and median attributed_value per action type. Use it as the fallback tier in buildOutcomeAdjustment when workspace availability is 'no_data'/'degraded' (extend the availability contract in docs/rules/outcome-learning-default-path.md), and expose a 'portfolio benchmark' line in LearningsSlice for prompts and the client briefing ('this kind of fix typically pays off within N weeks for sites like yours').

findings.title: predicted_emv accrues on every rec completion but realized-vs-predicted calibration is unimplemented
findings.detail: Verified tracked_actions.predicted_emv is written on rec completion (server/outcome-tracking.ts:215, routes/recommendations.ts:216) and getCalibrationOutcomes already SELECTs it alongside attributed_value (outcome-tracking.ts:100), but server/scoring/ov-calibration.ts:14-36 explicitly documents that calibration still uses the win-rate proxy and that 'realized-EMV-vs-predicted-EMV' is the deferred P6 basis. The data needed to answer 'are our dollar projections honest?' — and to make the OV scores that rank every client recommendation trustworthy — is being collected and ignored. Compounding it, outcome-backfill.ts:210 nulls predictedEmv for all platform-executed completions, so the pairing dataset accrues mostly from the rare manual-PATCH path.
findings.recommendation: Implement the documented P6 basis: median(attributedValue / predictedEmv) per (actionType, bucket) inside computeOvCalibration, gated on a minimum sample size with fallback to the current win-rate proxy. Fix the pairing leak first by snapshotting predictedEmv at auto-resolve/in-place-resolve time (recommendations.ts:463, :2295) so platform-executed completions contribute pairs.

findings.title: earlySignal (7-day on-track indicator) is captured for every action and rendered nowhere
findings.detail: Verified zero matches for 'earlySignal' anywhere in src/. The server computes a 7-day early signal per tracked action and broadcasts it in OUTCOME_SCORED, but both the admin OutcomeActionFeed and all client surfaces show pending actions as a 30-90 day black box. For clients especially, 'we shipped this 8 days ago and it is already trending up' is the cheapest possible trust signal during the dead period before checkpoint scoring.
findings.recommendation: Render earlySignal as a small badge on OutcomeActionFeed rows (admin) and on WinsSurface/pending items (client): on_track=emerald, no_movement=zinc, too_early=hidden. The data is already in the OUTCOME_SCORED broadcast and the action row; this is pure frontend work plus one field on the public wins/summary serializers.

findings.title: Per-keyword rank history (180 days retained) never powers client progress on requested keywords
findings.detail: rank_snapshots retains 180 days of daily GSC rank data per workspace (server/rank-tracking.ts:89-90 prunes to 180) but its only UI consumer is the admin KeywordDetailDrawer sparkline. Client-requested keywords carry CLIENT_REQUESTED provenance and auto-create tracked keywords with baselines (server/routes/public-portal.ts:354-360), yet verified no client component references CLIENT_REQUESTED — a client who requests a keyword gets no 'you asked for X, here is its trend since we started tracking it' loop. Tracked-keyword baselines (baselinePosition/baselineClicks/baselineImpressions) similarly have no consumer outside rank-tracking modules, so keyword-level 'did this pay off' is unanswerable despite the data existing.
findings.recommendation: Two unlocks from the same store: (1) a client StrategyTab card listing client-requested/approved keywords with baseline-vs-current position from getLatestSnapshotRanks + history (data already exposed via the public rank-tracking /history endpoint); (2) a keyword-level outcome bridge — recordAction on track/promote with the existing baseline fields, scored against rank_snapshots position deltas instead of page-level GSC trends, closing the Hub→Action Results gap both maps flag as MISSING.

findings.title: Keyword Hub's assembled read model (lifecycle, value scores, dollar values) is invisible to all AI consumers
findings.detail: Verified server/intelligence/seo-context-slice.ts exposes only a thin rankTracking aggregate (count/avgPosition/positionChanges, ~lines 113-141, one prompt line in formatters.ts). The Hub computes per-keyword lifecycle status (needs_review/retired/declined), Layer-1 value scores, keywordDollarValue ($/mo), variant rollups, and raw-evidence universe for the UI (server/keyword-command-center.ts) — none of it reaches any slice. AdminChat cannot answer 'which keywords need review' or 'top opportunity by value' despite the server computing exactly that on every page load. This is a partial Data Flow Rule 6 violation per CLAUDE.md.
findings.recommendation: Add a keywordLifecycle summary to SeoContextSlice (or a slim new slice): counts per lifecycle status, top-5 opportunities by value score with dollar value, and needs_review queue size. Reuse the existing skinny build path's count machinery rather than the full row build to keep slice assembly cheap. This also gives the strategy-generation prompt a value-ranked candidate signal it currently lacks.

findings.title: strategy_history stores 5 snapshots per workspace; 4 are write-only — strategy evolution is untrendable
findings.detail: Verified every read path uses LIMIT 1 / latest-only: server/keyword-strategy-ux.ts:55 (LIMIT 1), server/routes/keyword-strategy.ts:303 (diff route, LIMIT 1), server/intelligence/seo-context-slice.ts:25 (reads generated_at only). The persist transaction snapshots full strategy_json + page_map_json and retains 5 (keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:176-179), so coverage %, keyword counts, and avg-position trajectories across generations are stored but unreachable. The AI cannot reason about whether past strategy changes worked, and the admin has no history browser despite a StrategyDiff component existing for latest-vs-current.
findings.recommendation: Add a strategy-evolution endpoint that maps the 5 retained snapshots to a compact trend (generatedAt, keyword count, pages mapped, avg position, gaps open/closed) and render it as a small trend strip atop the admin Analysis tab; feed the same compact series into the seoContext slice so strategy regeneration prompts can see 'last 3 generations: coverage 42%→58%→61%'. The history data already round-trips through Zod schemas (server/schemas/workspace-schemas.ts:175-197).

findings.title: searchSignals blob (device/country mix, period comparison, GA4 organic overview) persisted every generation, zero consumers
findings.detail: Verified: grep for searchSignals in src/ returns nothing; the only server references are the write (server/keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:115) and assembler pass-through (server/keyword-strategy-assembler.ts:90). Every generation pays GSC/GA4 API calls to build device/country breakdowns, 28-day period comparison, and top organic landing pages, then ships them in the workspace blob and every admin GET response as dead weight.
findings.recommendation: Either render it or stop persisting it. The render option is cheap and client-valuable: a 'Search Snapshot' SectionCard (device/country split + period delta) on the admin Analysis tab and/or client Performance tab — the data is already in the strategy payload both fetch. If not rendered within a cycle, strip it from the persist path to shrink every blob and response.

findings.title: AI review verdicts and the claim-evidence ledger are discarded after each run; scraped source text is discarded after brief generation
findings.detail: Verified server/routes/content-posts.ts:570-581 returns the parsed AI review (per-item verdicts + claimsToVerify + evidence pairing) to the UI only — nothing persists it, so closing PostEditor loses the review, re-running re-spends tokens, and there is no audit trail that review happened before approval. Relatedly, ScrapedPage content fetched for SERP/reference grounding at brief time is used once and dropped, which is why the evidence ledger (server/content-review-evidence-ledger.ts) must match claims against title/URL token overlap instead of source text.
findings.recommendation: Persist the review result on content_posts (a reviewed_at + review_json column or a content_post_reviews table) — unlocks: review audit trail, client-visible 'what was verified' on PostReviewCard, and per-workspace analytics on which checklist items fail most (a free quality-eval fixture source). Separately store scraped reference/SERP text on content_briefs (or a side table) so the deterministic ledger can do real-text claim matching.

findings.title: Client edit/steering signals on posts are collected but never analyzed; the module built to analyze them is dead code
findings.detail: content_post_versions tags every snapshot with trigger detail including client_edit (verified: server/routes/public-content.ts:786-798 coalescing, server/content-posts-db.ts:417), and clients submit steering feedback text on request-changes — yet nothing computes '% of sections clients rewrite', which section types get regenerated, or recurring tone complaints. server/copy-voice-feedback.ts (steering-note voice classification → guardrail suggestions) has zero importers (verified by the surface map; module exists). Post-level voiceScore columns also never aggregate: workspace-learnings.ts:173-188 only computes voiceScoreCorrelation from voice_calibrated actions whose voice_score metric is never present in current snapshots, so it is permanently null.
findings.recommendation: Wire the existing copy-voice-feedback.ts classifier into the request-changes path (post + copy pipeline) to accumulate voice-guardrail suggestions per workspace, and add a cheap version-trigger rollup (client_edit count / total sections per post) into ContentPipelineSlice. Both feed the brand engine the founder already built — this is connection work, not new collection.

findings.title: GenerationQuality telemetry is computed then only logged — generation health is invisible and eval fixtures are thrown away
findings.detail: Verified server/keyword-strategy-generation.ts:588-606 builds a typed GenerationQuality object (pool size, suppressed hallucinations, backfilled gap count, floor hits — shared/types/generation-quality.ts) and emits it only via log.info(:602); it is returned from the synthesis function but no caller persists it. Generation-quality regressions (e.g. sanitizer suddenly suppressing 40% of output, backfill floor constantly hit) are detectable only by log spelunking, and the AI-quality-evals pipeline (docs/rules/ai-quality-evals.md) gets no fixtures from production runs.
findings.recommendation: Persist one row per generation (workspace_id, generated_at, GenerationQuality JSON) — a tiny append-only table. Unlocks: a cross-workspace generation-health panel on the existing admin overview, alerting when suppressedCount/poolSize ratio spikes, and deterministic eval fixtures harvested from real generations per the AI reliability registry pattern.

findings.title: GA4 conversion data is fetched on every strategy generation for the prompt, then discarded
findings.detail: Verified server/keyword-strategy-search-data.ts:31,68-97 fetches ga4Conversions and events-by-page per generation and returns them only into the synthesis prompt; nothing in keyword-strategy-persistence.ts writes them (searchSignals carries only an organic overview). Conversion-weighted prioritization — which pages/keywords actually convert, not just rank — is computed once per generation and thrown away, so quick-win ROI scores and recommendation OV (which uses CPC proxies) never see real conversion weight.
findings.recommendation: Persist a compact conversions-by-page map alongside searchSignals (or in a small table) at generation time, then consume it in two cheap places: quick-win roiScore weighting in keyword-strategy-helpers, and computeOpportunityValue's EMV basis as a conversion-rate refinement over the CTR-curve proxy. The fetch cost is already paid every generation.

findings.title: competitorKeywordData is paid for and persisted but has no UI; CompetitiveIntel re-fetches the same intelligence on a separate cache
findings.detail: Verified the blob's competitorKeywordData (<=150 rows, persisted at server/keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:109) IS reused server-side as a fetch-avoidance cache for the synthesis keyword pool (server/keyword-strategy-seo-data.ts:108-109) — but no frontend reads it; src/components/strategy/CompetitiveIntel.tsx fetches its own data from /api/seo/competitive-intel with an independent 48h cache. Two storage paths for the same paid DataForSEO intelligence, and the 150 rows the strategy actually grounded on are invisible to the admin.
findings.recommendation: Make CompetitiveIntel render the persisted competitorKeywordData as its instant first paint (with live re-fetch as refresh), and add a 'competitor keywords you could target' module that cross-filters those 150 rows against the keyword universe's declined/branded filters — surfacing exactly the rows the AI saw. Also exposes provenance: admin can verify what grounded the strategy.

findings.title: Client-requested keywords reach the admin only as a count; briefing/digest story engagement is never captured
findings.detail: Two ends of the same client-signal pipe are unexploited. Inbound: verified src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx:299,422 shows requested/approved keyword feedback only as counts ('N declined · N requested · N approved') with no list and no action — a client request sits invisible until the next full AI regeneration happens to honor the prompt hint. Outbound: verified zero tracking of which briefing/digest stories clients open (no storyClick/story_view/trackStory references anywhere), so ClientSignalsSlice — the platform's best slice — cannot learn which story types drive engagement, and the briefing generator optimizes blind.
findings.recommendation: Inbound: render the requested-keyword list in the admin feedback card with a one-click 'add to strategy' that writes page_keywords directly (the MCP tool add_keyword_to_strategy already implements this write path — reuse it as a route). Outbound: add a lightweight POST /api/public/briefing/:id/engagement (story id + event) feeding a new ClientSignalsSlice.briefingEngagement field; the slice and invalidation plumbing already exist, this is one endpoint + one slice field.

analyses.summary: The platform's growth fundamentals are stronger than typical for a solo-founder codebase: WAL-mode SQLite with foreign keys, a real background-job platform used by every heavy generation path, state machines enforced at the store layer (POST_STATUS_TRANSITIONS, RECOMMENDATION_TRANSITIONS, TRACKED_KEYWORD_TRANSITIONS all verified in code), a genuine coverage ratchet (61.33% line floor, 473 integration test files), and a normalization playbook (migrations 088-090) that was actually executed for keyword-strategy arrays. The growth risks are concentrated in four places: (1) two stores that violate the platform's own normalization rule and will hurt first at 10x — recommendation_sets as a single per-workspace JSON blob rewritten on every status flip, and the rank_tracking_config blob still serving as the read path a migration after the tracked_keywords row table shipped; (2) per-request O(everything) read paths — the 3,563-line Keyword Command Center read model rebuilt from scratch on every pagination click and the outcomes overview's O(workspaces × actions) query loop; (3) an in-flight migration backlog of three parallel keyword shells and two parallel client overviews held open by five staging-validation flags, doubling the maintenance surface of two of the six audited surfaces; (4) the content bounded context, where domain extraction has barely started (one file in server/domains/content vs sixteen in domains/inbox) and a 1,021-line route file owns AI review, claim extraction, and a fire-and-forget Webflow publish. Fix-before-building-more: recommendation storage identity, the outcome-learning data-quality layer, and the keyword-hub P5 cutover. Fine-for-now: SQLite itself, rank_snapshots day-blob design (180-day prune exists), and test coverage.

findings.title: recommendation_sets violates the platform's own migrations-088-090 normalization rule — and the P5/P6 calibration roadmap is being built on unstable rec identity
findings.detail: recommendation_sets is one row per workspace with the entire rec array in a TEXT column (server/db/migrations/003-per-workspace.sql:126, read/write at server/recommendations.ts:375-402). Every client status flip re-serializes and rewrites the whole set (updateRecommendationStatus at server/recommendations.ts:415-437 calls saveRecommendations on the full array), and at least three other consumers full-parse the blob (server/outcome-backfill.ts:155-170, operational slice, briefing candidates). Worse for growth: the merge preserves ids only for in_progress/completed/dismissed recs, so pending recs get fresh random ids on every regen — any future per-rec feature (dismissal-reason learning, rec age/recurrence tracking, predicted-vs-realized EMV pairing planned for P6) has no stable key to attach to. CLAUDE.md's own rule says 'arrays that need filtering or sorting belong in dedicated tables'; this is the largest remaining violation among the six surfaces.
findings.recommendation: Schedule a migrations-088-090-style extraction: a recommendations table (id, workspace_id, type, status, priority, page refs, opportunity JSON, created_at/updated_at) with deterministic ids derived from (type + source key) so identity survives regens, plus a summary column or view. Do this BEFORE building P5 effort-calibration or P6 EMV-realization features — both need stable per-rec rows. The existing assembleStoredKeywordStrategy table-as-truth/blob-fallback pattern is the proven template.

findings.title: Tracked-keywords dual-write shadow has no scheduled cutover — two stores for the same data with reads still on the JSON blob
findings.detail: Migration 118 created the indexed tracked_keywords row table, and server/tracked-keywords-store.ts dual-writes it, but the module's own comments confirm the table is write-only: 'the GENERAL read path (getTrackedKeywords + the public serializers) must' stay on the blob, and the backfill 'does NOT strip the blob (shadow, not strip) and does NOT switch any read' (server/tracked-keywords-store.ts:88, 324, 478-481). All reads still parse rank_tracking_config.tracked_keywords (server/rank-tracking.ts:227-236 normalizes the full list on every mutation). Indefinite dual-store periods are where drift bugs incubate — every new tracked-keyword field must now be added in two shapes, and the CAS/write-lock machinery (tracked-keywords-store.ts:478-532) is permanent complexity that only exists to keep the shadow consistent.
findings.recommendation: Tie the read-path cutover to the keyword-hub P5 flag flip (they touch the same surface and the same validation window): switch getTrackedKeywords and the public serializers to the row table, run a one-release verification compare, then strip the blob and delete the dual-write/CAS machinery. Add the cutover as an explicit roadmap item so the shadow doesn't become permanent.

findings.title: Keyword Command Center read model is rebuilt from scratch on every request — a known scaling cliff the team has instrumented but not capped
findings.detail: server/routes/keyword-command-center.ts:86-115 calls buildKeywordCommandCenterSummary/Rows/Detail directly with no server-side cache; the only caching in the 3,563-line builder is the prepared-statement cache and a WeakMap variant-parent index (server/keyword-command-center.ts:1965-1972). Every pagination/sort/filter click re-runs the six-source merge (strategy tables, tracked keywords, GSC snapshots, feedback, local SEO, discovered queries) plus per-row finalize. The code itself anticipates the problem — per-stage heap/timing telemetry at keyword-command-center.ts:1524, 1635, and a UNIVERSE_SAFETY_CEILING of 2000 rows (line 98) that activates with the keyword-universe-full flag — so with that flag ON, each click of Previous/Next on a large workspace evaluates up to 2,000 merged rows server-side. At 10x workspaces with concurrent admin sessions, this is the most expensive synchronous read path in the platform.
findings.recommendation: Before the P5 flag flip, add a short-TTL per-workspace memo of the assembled row model keyed by (workspaceId, universe inputs hash), invalidated by the same mutation hooks that already call invalidateIntelligenceCache (keyword-command-center.ts:3354, 3448 prove the invalidation seam exists). Pagination/sort/filter should then operate on the cached model rather than re-merging sources. This is a contained change because the route adapter is already thin.

findings.title: GET /api/outcomes/overview is an O(workspaces × actions) query loop — degrades linearly-times-linearly with growth
findings.detail: server/routes/outcomes.ts:145-193 iterates every workspace and per workspace runs getWorkspaceCounts, getTopWinsForWorkspace, computeScorecard, then getActionsByWorkspace AND a per-action getOutcomesForAction loop just to count scoredLast30d. That is roughly 3×W×A queries for one admin page render, all synchronous better-sqlite3 calls on the main thread (blocking the event loop for every other request while it runs). With 10x workspaces each accumulating daily-cron-scored actions, this page becomes the slowest admin surface and a head-of-line blocker for the whole server.
findings.recommendation: Replace the per-action loop with one aggregate SQL (JOIN tracked_actions to action_outcomes, GROUP BY workspace_id with COUNT(DISTINCT action_id) FILTER measured_at >= date) and fold counts/win-rate into the same pass. Small, isolated, high-leverage; no schema change needed.

findings.title: Five staging-validation flags hold open three parallel keyword shells and two parallel client overviews — the migration backlog is the biggest maintenance-cost multiplier among the six surfaces
findings.detail: shared/types/feature-flags.ts shows keyword-hub, keyword-universe-full, and keyword-value-scoring all at rolloutTarget 'staging-validation' (created 2026-06-02, weekly stale-audit cadence), and client-briefing-v2 / client-briefing-v2-ai-polish at 'tiered-client-rollout' (created 2026-04-28, monthly cadence). Concretely that means production maintains KeywordCommandCenter.tsx (663 lines) + RankTracker.tsx (623 lines) while staging validates KeywordHub.tsx (511 lines) — three shells for one surface — and the legacy OverviewTab+InsightsDigest (~1,270 lines) coexists with the Briefing suite (~1,500 lines), with the weekly briefing cron explicitly gated dark for flag-off workspaces (server/briefing-cron.ts:537-542). The flag hygiene itself is exemplary (owners, removal conditions, audit cadences all present), but five concurrent dual-implementation surfaces means every keyword or overview change is written and tested 2-3 times, and the briefing-v2 flag is now 6+ weeks old with monthly cadence — drifting toward a permanent fork.
findings.recommendation: Treat cutovers as features: prioritize the keyword-hub P5 cutover (flip flags, fold seo-ranks in, delete legacy KCC + RankTracker shells and the crude computeOpportunityScore path per the flags' own removalConditions) before adding any new keyword-surface capability. Set a decision date for client-briefing-v2 (promote to default or kill) — the dark briefing data pipeline means most clients get zero value from code that runs weekly. Note the Hub also has launch blockers to fix during cutover (no manual add-keyword input when flag is ON; protected-action force:true bypass).

findings.title: Content bounded context extraction has barely started: 1,021-line route file owns AI review, claim extraction, and an inline fire-and-forget Webflow publish
findings.detail: server/domains/content/ contains exactly one file (send-post-to-client.ts) while server/domains/inbox/ has sixteen — proof the route-to-service pattern works here but was never applied to content. server/routes/content-posts.ts (1,021 lines) carries the AI review checklist orchestration, numeric-claim extraction, ai-fix flows, AND an ~80-line fire-and-forget .then() auto-publish chain inside the PATCH handler (lines 369-441, verified) with an inline hatch comment admitting the debt: '// background-generation-ok: legacy auto-publish follow-up; Phase 2 keeps this visible through jobs or a domain queue.' Consequences beyond style: publish failure is only a log.warn (admin sees Approved, publish silently failed), the auto-publish path skips queueKeywordStrategyPostUpdateFollowOns that the manual route runs (server/routes/content-publish.ts:224), and two divergent copies of field-mapping/publish logic now exist and have already drifted (summary/featuredImage mapping missing from the inline copy).
findings.recommendation: Extract a single publishPostToWebflow service into server/domains/content/ used by both routes, and run auto-publish through the background-job platform (it already meets the docs/rules/background-generation.md criteria: external API calls continuing after response). This is 'fix before building more' — every new content feature currently lands in the route file and inherits the divergence.

findings.title: Outcome-learning data quality must be fixed before wiring more consumers to learnings — the backfill cron actively corrupts the calibration signal the platform multiplies scores by
findings.detail: Verified: server/outcome-backfill.ts:196 hardcodes actionType 'audit_fix_applied' for every completed recommendation it backfills, even though the rec object (with rec.type) is available in the same loop and a compile-time-exhaustive recommendationOutcomeActionType mapping exists for exactly this purpose (server/recommendations.ts:198-242). Because platform-executed completions (auto-resolve, work-order/approval applies) never call recordAction directly, this weekly cron is the dominant path creating their tracked actions — so winRateByActionType, the signal that buildOutcomeAdjustment uses to multiply recommendation and keyword scores (clamped 0.75-1.25, server/outcome-learning-default-path.ts), is systematically mislabeled. Combined with the mapper-level scoring defects (non-existent primary metrics for ~6/15 action types; not_acted_on suggestion rows scored as executed), the platform is scaling consumers (rec ranking, keyword candidate scoring, strategy prompts, OV calibration) on a corrupted learning substrate. From a growth perspective this is the worst kind of debt: it compounds silently as more surfaces subscribe to learnings.
findings.recommendation: Fix-before-building-more, in order: (1) backfill uses recommendationOutcomeActionType(rec.type); (2) filter attribution='not_acted_on' out of getPendingMeasurement and computeWorkspaceLearnings; (3) either implement or delete the phantom primary metrics (click_recovery, target_improvement, content_produced, page_health_score-vs-GSC mismatch). Freeze any new learnings consumers until these land — the clamps bound the damage but also mean every consumer added now is calibrated on noise.

findings.title: ClientDashboard.tsx is an 842-line shell firing ~20 query hooks at bootstrap regardless of active tab — the landing spot for every new client feature
findings.detail: src/components/ClientDashboard.tsx contains ~92 hook call sites (842 lines) and mounts copy entries, content plan, pricing, page keywords, annotations, and anomalies queries on the overview regardless of which tab the client opens; the anomalies query survives solely to feed chat context after its UI consumer was removed (ClientDashboard.tsx:26 comment). Each of those hooks hits a public endpoint that runs synchronous SQLite reads, so 10x concurrent clients means 10x × ~20 bootstrap requests against a single-threaded better-sqlite3 process. The component is also the routing point for the briefing-v2 fork, so the god-component problem and the dual-overview problem compound each other.
findings.recommendation: Move tab-specific hooks into their tab components (most already exist as separate files) with enabled gating on active tab, and let React Query's cache handle tab-switch warmth. Cheap to do incrementally; should be a precondition for the briefing-v2 cutover so the new overview doesn't inherit the eager-fetch pattern.

findings.title: workspaces.keyword_strategy blob carries write-only payloads (searchSignals, competitorKeywordData, 4 of 5 history snapshots) — write amplification on the hottest row in the database
findings.detail: Verified by grep: searchSignals has zero consumers anywhere in src/, yet it is rebuilt and persisted into the workspaces.keyword_strategy JSON column on every generation (server/keyword-strategy-persistence.ts:115-121); competitorKeywordData (up to 150 provider-credit-paid rows, persistence:109) is likewise never read by any UI — CompetitiveIntel re-fetches its own data with a separate 48h cache. strategy_history retains 5 snapshots per workspace but only the latest is ever read (diff route). The workspaces row is the single most-read row per workspace (every intelligence slice assembly, every admin GET, every client strategy read parses the blob), so dead fields directly tax every read path and inflate the WAL on every generation.
findings.recommendation: Fine-for-now functionally, but cheap to clean: either render these payloads (the surface maps identify free wins — a search-snapshot card, competitor-keyword fallback, strategy-evolution trend) or stop persisting them in the same release. Decide per-field; don't let the blob keep accreting on the strip-to-tables trajectory the team already established in Wave 3b.

findings.title: rank_snapshots day-blob design is fine for now (180-day prune exists) but the per-keyword history read parses every day-blob — flag for the Hub's growth path, not for immediate work
findings.detail: rank_snapshots stores up to 5,000 GSC queries as one JSON blob per workspace-day, with a verified retention prune to 180 days (server/rank-tracking.ts:88-91). However the history read path is 'SELECT * FROM rank_snapshots WHERE workspace_id = ? ORDER BY date ASC' (rank-tracking.ts:75) — building one keyword's sparkline parses up to 180 × 5,000 query objects. Today only the drawer sparkline pays this cost; but three roadmap-adjacent features (keyword-level outcome measurement, client requested-keyword progress cards, lost-visibility insights) would all read per-keyword history, multiplying this scan.
findings.recommendation: Ignore until a second consumer of per-keyword history is built; at that point extract a normalized per-tracked-keyword daily rank table (keyword_key, workspace_id, date, position, clicks, impressions) fed by the same scheduler — tracked keywords only (hundreds of rows/day, not 5,000), leaving the raw day-blobs as the archive. The tracked_keywords row table from migration 118 is the natural foreign anchor.

findings.title: ~2,600 lines of dead-but-maintained code across the six surfaces actively misleads agents and inflates every refactor
findings.detail: Verified zero importers for: src/components/client/OutcomeSummary.tsx (277 lines), WeCalledIt.tsx (228), FixRecommendations.tsx (616, recently updated with P4 contract comments), and server/copy-voice-feedback.ts (the steering-note voice-learning loop). The public GET /api/public/outcomes/:id/summary endpoint and useClientOutcomeSummary hook have no live UI consumer. In an AI-agent-driven codebase this is worse than ordinary dead code: CLAUDE.md's session protocol tells agents to 'search the codebase to verify a component doesn't already exist' — these files answer 'it exists' for features that don't actually ship, and maintenance passes (like the P4 comment updates on FixRecommendations) keep burning effort on them.
findings.recommendation: One cleanup PR with an explicit decision per file: mount it (the outcome scorecard is the strongest candidate — it is the commercial proof-of-value surface and the endpoint already works) or delete it with a roadmap note. Do not leave 'built, never mounted' as a third state; the deprecation-lifecycle taxonomy in docs/rules/deprecation-lifecycle.md exists for exactly this.

findings.title: Test coverage of the six surfaces is genuinely healthy — do not schedule broad coverage work; spend the testing budget on the two verified gap patterns instead
findings.detail: Verified: 473 integration test files + 744 unit test files, with dedicated coverage of all six surfaces (keyword-hub-actions, keyword-hub-list, keyword-command-center-routes/assembler-swap, keyword-strategy-admin-assembler/public-read, recommendation edge routes, outcomes read/edge routes, content-posts lifecycle/ai-fix/workflow, briefing dual-write). The coverage ratchet is real and enforced at 61.33% lines / 49.44% branches (docs/testing/coverage-ratchet-ci.md). Two real gaps: (1) docs/testing/critical-domain-coverage-baseline.md still shows a stale 32.31% global baseline from 2026-05-13 vs the ratchet's 2026-06-04 baseline — doc drift in a repo whose rules treat doc truth as load-bearing; (2) the SEO Strategy map documents a guard test that masks a real regression by seeding identical data into both the blob and the table (tests/integration/keyword-strategy-admin-assembler.test.ts:41-53), i.e. dual-store tests must seed DIVERGENT fixtures to prove which store wins — a pattern worth a test-convention rule given two more dual-store cutovers (tracked keywords, recommendation_sets) are coming.
findings.recommendation: Refresh the stale baseline doc, and add a test convention (CLAUDE.md or pr-check) requiring divergent-fixture seeding for any table-as-truth/blob-fallback assertion. Skip any broader 'improve coverage' initiative for these surfaces — the ratchet already does that job.

findings.title: Polling is residual, not systemic — the background-job + WS event architecture is correctly adopted; only minor stragglers remain
findings.detail: All heavy generation across the six surfaces runs through server/jobs.ts (strategy generation, recommendation regen, brief/post generation, copy batches) with WS broadcast + React Query invalidation as the freshness mechanism — the architecture the question asks about is in place. Verified residual polling: useAdminPosts polls at 3-5s while a post status is 'generating' (src/hooks/admin/useAdminPosts.ts:13-31), useAnomalyAlerts refetches every 10 minutes, useNotifications polls on an interval. The post-generation poll is the only one with real cost (per-section generation runs minutes, so a 3s poll per open editor adds steady load), and it duplicates information the job system already broadcasts.
findings.recommendation: Fine for now. When next touching the post editor, replace the generating-status poll with a POST_UPDATED/job-progress WS handler (the broadcast infrastructure and useWorkspaceEvents contract already exist per Data Flow Rule 2). Not worth a dedicated PR.
